Arxiv今日论文 | 2024-10-23

发表于2024-10-23|更新于2024-11-01|学术会议Arxiv
|字数总计:185.4k|阅读时长:960分钟|阅读量:|评论数:

本篇博文主要展示 2024-10-23 从Arxiv.org论文网站获取的最新论文列表，自动更新，按照NLP、CV、ML、AI、IR五个大方向区分，若需要邮件定时接收，请在评论区留下你的邮箱号。

说明：每日论文数据从Arxiv.org获取，每天早上11:00左右定时自动更新。

友情提示: 如何您需要邮箱接收每日论文数据，请在评论处留下你的邮箱。

概览 (2024-10-23)

今日共更新502篇论文,其中:

  • 自然语言处理共103篇(Computation and Language (cs.CL))
  • 人工智能共137篇(Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI))

  • 机器学习共172篇(Machine Learning (cs.LG))

自然语言处理

[NLP-0] Altogether: Image Captioning via Re-aligning Alt-text EMNLP2024 ACL

【速读】： 该论文试图解决现有图像描述生成方法的两个主要问题：一是忽略现有图像的替代文本（alt-text）元数据，二是缺乏对描述生成模型训练数据的透明度。解决方案的关键在于提出了一种基于现有alt-text进行编辑和重新对齐的系统方法——Altogether。该方法通过多轮人工注释，使注释者基于现有alt-text逐步调整并重新对齐到图像内容，从而生成包含丰富视觉概念的描述。这种方法不仅区别于以往仅依赖图像和注释者知识的一次性描述任务，还通过训练一个能够大规模重新对齐alt-text的描述生成模型，显著提升了图像描述的质量，并改进了文本到图像生成和零样本图像分类任务的性能。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17251
作者: Hu Xu,Po-Yao Huang,Xiaoqing Ellen Tan,Ching-Feng Yeh,Jacob Kahn,Christine Jou,Gargi Ghosh,Omer Levy,Luke Zettlemoyer,Wen-tau Yih,Shang-Wen Li,Saining Xie,Christoph Feichtenhofer
关键词-EN: creating synthetic data, focuses on creating, creating synthetic, paper focuses, Existing
类目: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: accepted by EMNLP 2024; MetaCLIPv2

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This paper focuses on creating synthetic data to improve the quality of image captions. Existing works typically have two shortcomings. First, they caption images from scratch, ignoring existing alt-text metadata, and second, lack transparency if the captioners’ training data (e.g. GPT) is unknown. In this paper, we study a principled approach Altogether based on the key idea to edit and re-align existing alt-texts associated with the images. To generate training data, we perform human annotation where annotators start with the existing alt-text and re-align it to the image content in multiple rounds, consequently constructing captions with rich visual concepts. This differs from prior work that carries out human annotation as a one-time description task solely based on images and annotator knowledge. We train a captioner on this data that generalizes the process of re-aligning alt-texts at scale. Our results show our Altogether approach leads to richer image captions that also improve text-to-image generation and zero-shot image classification tasks.
摘要：本文聚焦于通过生成合成数据来提升图像描述的质量。现有工作通常存在两个缺点。首先，它们从零开始为图像添加描述，忽略了现有的替代文本 (alt-text) 元数据；其次，如果描述生成器的训练数据 (例如 GPT) 未知，则缺乏透明度。本文研究了一种基于关键思想的系统方法 Altogether，该方法通过编辑和重新对齐与图像相关联的现有替代文本。为了生成训练数据，我们进行了人工标注，标注者从现有的替代文本开始，经过多轮调整，使其与图像内容重新对齐，从而构建出包含丰富视觉概念的描述。这与以往将人工标注作为一次性描述任务的工作不同，后者仅基于图像和标注者的知识进行标注。我们基于此数据训练了一个描述生成器，该生成器能够大规模地重新对齐替代文本。实验结果表明，我们的 Altogether 方法生成的图像描述更为丰富，同时还能提升文本到图像的生成以及零样本图像分类任务的性能。

[NLP-1] JMMMU: A Japanese Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding Benchmark for Culture-aware Evaluation

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决非英语语言（特别是日语）中大型多模态模型（LMMs）的研究加速问题，以提升更广泛用户群体的体验。解决方案的关键在于引入了JMMMU（Japanese MMMU），这是首个大规模的日语基准测试，专门设计用于评估LMMs在基于日本文化背景的专家级任务上的表现。JMMMU包含两个互补子集：文化无关（CA）子集和文化特定（CS）子集。CA子集通过将文化无关的科目（如数学）翻译成日语，实现与英语MMMU的一对一比较；CS子集则包含反映日本文化背景的新创科目。通过这两个子集的结合，论文揭示了LMMs在日语环境下的性能下降，并暴露了其在日本文化理解上的不足，从而为多语言LMM的发展提供了高标准、文化多样性的基准测试指南。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17250
作者: Shota Onohara,Atsuyuki Miyai,Yuki Imajuku,Kazuki Egashira,Jeonghun Baek,Xiang Yue,Graham Neubig,Kiyoharu Aizawa
关键词-EN: Large Multimodal Models, Multimodal Models, Large Multimodal, enhancing user experiences, Accelerating research
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
备注: Project page: this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Accelerating research on Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) in non-English languages is crucial for enhancing user experiences across broader populations. In this paper, we introduce JMMMU (Japanese MMMU), the first large-scale Japanese benchmark designed to evaluate LMMs on expert-level tasks based on the Japanese cultural context. To facilitate comprehensive culture-aware evaluation, JMMMU features two complementary subsets: (i) culture-agnostic (CA) subset, where the culture-independent subjects (e.g., Math) are selected and translated into Japanese, enabling one-to-one comparison with its English counterpart MMMU; and (ii) culture-specific (CS) subset, comprising newly crafted subjects that reflect Japanese cultural context. Using the CA subset, we observe performance drop in many LMMs when evaluated in Japanese, which is purely attributable to language variation. Using the CS subset, we reveal their inadequate Japanese cultural understanding. Further, by combining both subsets, we identify that some LMMs perform well on the CA subset but not on the CS subset, exposing a shallow understanding of the Japanese language that lacks depth in cultural understanding. We hope this work will not only help advance LMM performance in Japanese but also serve as a guideline to create high-standard, culturally diverse benchmarks for multilingual LMM development. The project page is this https URL.
摘要：加速非英语语言的大规模多模态模型 (LMM) 研究对于提升更广泛用户群体的体验至关重要。本文介绍了 JMMMU (Japanese MMMU)，这是首个针对基于日本文化背景的专家级任务评估 LMM 的大规模日语基准。为了促进全面的文化感知评估，JMMMU 包含两个互补的子集：(i) 文化无关 (CA) 子集，其中选择了与文化无关的主题（例如数学）并翻译成日语，使得可以与英语对应的 MMMU 进行一对一比较；以及 (ii) 文化特定 (CS) 子集，包含反映日本文化背景的新创主题。通过 CA 子集，我们观察到许多 LMM 在日语评估中的性能下降，这纯粹归因于语言差异。通过 CS 子集，我们揭示了它们对日本文化理解不足的问题。进一步，通过结合两个子集，我们发现某些 LMM 在 CA 子集上表现良好，但在 CS 子集上表现不佳，暴露出对日语的浅层理解，缺乏深入的文化理解。我们希望这项工作不仅有助于提升 LMM 在日语中的性能，还能为创建高标准、文化多样化的多语言 LMM 开发基准提供指导。项目页面为 https URL。

[NLP-2] PyramidDrop: Accelerating Your Large Vision-Language Models via Pyramid Visual Redundancy Reduction

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大规模视觉语言模型（LVLMs）中图像输入导致的计算成本高昂问题。解决方案的关键在于提出了一种名为PyramidDrop的视觉冗余减少策略。具体来说，PyramidDrop通过在模型的不同阶段按预定义比例丢弃部分图像tokens，形成金字塔状的视觉tokens分布，从而在不显著影响模型性能的前提下，大幅提升训练和推理效率。该方法的核心在于在模型浅层保留所有视觉tokens，而在深层逐步减少冗余tokens，并通过轻量级的相似度计算实现token的智能丢弃，实验证明该策略能够在保持性能的同时，实现显著的训练时间和推理计算量的减少。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17247
作者: Long Xing,Qidong Huang,Xiaoyi Dong,Jiajie Lu,Pan Zhang,Yuhang Zang,Yuhang Cao,Conghui He,Jiaqi Wang,Feng Wu,Dahua Lin
关键词-EN: large vision-language models, large vision-language, carry a wealth, image, image tokens
类目: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 10 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In large vision-language models (LVLMs), images serve as inputs that carry a wealth of information. As the idiom “A picture is worth a thousand words” implies, representing a single image in current LVLMs can require hundreds or even thousands of tokens. This results in significant computational costs, which grow quadratically as input image resolution increases, thereby severely impacting the efficiency of both training and inference. Previous approaches have attempted to reduce the number of image tokens either before or within the early layers of LVLMs. However, these strategies inevitably result in the loss of crucial image information, ultimately diminishing model performance. To address this challenge, we conduct an empirical study revealing that all visual tokens are necessary for LVLMs in the shallow layers, and token redundancy progressively increases in the deeper layers of the model. To this end, we propose PyramidDrop, a visual redundancy reduction strategy for LVLMs to boost their efficiency in both training and inference with neglectable performance loss. Specifically, we partition the LVLM into several stages and drop part of the image tokens at the end of each stage with a pre-defined ratio, creating pyramid-like visual tokens across model layers. The dropping is based on a lightweight similarity calculation with a negligible time overhead. Extensive experiments demonstrate that PyramidDrop can achieve a 40% training time and 55% inference FLOPs acceleration of LLaVA-NeXT with comparable performance. Besides, the PyramidDrop could also serve as a plug-and-play strategy for inference acceleration without training, with better performance and lower inference cost than counterparts. We hope that the insights and approach introduced by PyramidDrop will inspire future research to further investigate the role of image tokens in LVLMs.
摘要：在大规模视觉语言模型 (LVLM) 中，图像作为输入承载了丰富的信息。正如俗语“一图胜千言”所暗示的，在当前的 LVLM 中，表示单张图像可能需要数百甚至数千个 Token。这导致了显著的计算成本，随着输入图像分辨率的增加，计算成本呈二次方增长，从而严重影响了训练和推理的效率。先前的研究尝试在 LVLM 的早期层之前或之内减少图像 Token 的数量。然而，这些策略不可避免地会导致关键图像信息的丢失，最终降低模型性能。为了应对这一挑战，我们进行了一项实证研究，揭示了在浅层中所有视觉 Token 对于 LVLM 都是必要的，而在模型的深层中 Token 冗余逐渐增加。基于此，我们提出了 PyramidDrop，一种用于 LVLM 的视觉冗余减少策略，以在忽略性能损失的情况下提高其训练和推理效率。具体而言，我们将 LVLM 划分为若干阶段，并在每个阶段的末尾以预定义的比例丢弃部分图像 Token，从而在模型层间形成类似金字塔的视觉 Token 结构。丢弃操作基于轻量级的相似度计算，几乎不增加时间开销。广泛的实验表明，PyramidDrop 可以使 LLaVA-NeXT 的训练时间减少 40%，推理 FLOPs 减少 55%，同时保持相当的性能。此外，PyramidDrop 还可以作为一种即插即用的推理加速策略，无需训练，其性能和推理成本均优于同类方法。我们希望 PyramidDrop 引入的见解和方法能够启发未来的研究进一步探讨图像 Token 在 LVLM 中的作用。

[NLP-3] owards Reliable Evaluation of Behavior Steering Interventions in LLMs NEURIPS2024

【速读】： 该论文试图解决当前表示工程方法在评估其对模型行为影响时缺乏客观、定量指标的问题。解决方案的关键在于提出了一套新的评估框架，该框架强调四个核心属性：（i）评估上下文应与下游任务高度相似；（ii）应考虑模型似然性；（iii）评估应允许跨不同目标行为的标准化比较；（iv）应提供基线比较。通过引入这一基于上述标准的评估流程，论文不仅提供了定量分析，还通过可视化手段展示了不同表示工程方法在引导模型行为（如真实性和可修正性）方面的有效性，发现某些干预措施的效果不如先前报告的那样显著。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17245
作者: Itamar Pres,Laura Ruis,Ekdeep Singh Lubana,David Krueger
关键词-EN: recently shown promise, enabling efficient steering, Representation engineering methods, recently shown, shown promise
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Accepted to the NeurIPS 2024 - Workshop on Foundation Model Interventions

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Representation engineering methods have recently shown promise for enabling efficient steering of model behavior. However, evaluation pipelines for these methods have primarily relied on subjective demonstrations, instead of quantitative, objective metrics. We aim to take a step towards addressing this issue by advocating for four properties missing from current evaluations: (i) contexts sufficiently similar to downstream tasks should be used for assessing intervention quality; (ii) model likelihoods should be accounted for; (iii) evaluations should allow for standardized comparisons across different target behaviors; and (iv) baseline comparisons should be offered. We introduce an evaluation pipeline grounded in these criteria, offering both a quantitative and visual analysis of how effectively a given method works. We use this pipeline to evaluate two representation engineering methods on how effectively they can steer behaviors such as truthfulness and corrigibility, finding that some interventions are less effective than previously reported.
摘要：表示工程方法近期显示出在实现模型行为高效引导方面的潜力。然而，这些方法的评估流程主要依赖于主观演示，而非定量的客观指标。我们旨在通过倡导当前评估中缺失的四项特性来解决这一问题：(i) 评估干预质量时应使用与下游任务足够相似的上下文；(ii) 应考虑模型似然性；(iii) 评估应允许对不同目标行为进行标准化比较；(iv) 应提供基线比较。我们基于这些标准引入了一个评估流程，提供了对给定方法效果的定量和视觉分析。我们使用此流程评估了两种表示工程方法在引导真实性和可修正性等行为方面的有效性，发现某些干预措施的效果不如先前报告的那样显著。

[NLP-4] SELA: Tree-Search Enhanced LLM Agents for Automated Machine Learning

【速读】： 该论文试图解决传统AutoML方法在模型选择和集成过程中生成的代码多样性低且优化不足的问题。解决方案的关键在于引入基于蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）的增强型LLM代理系统（SELA），通过将管道配置表示为树结构，使代理能够智能地进行实验并迭代优化策略，从而更有效地探索机器学习解决方案空间，提升解决方案的整体质量。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17238
作者: Yizhou Chi,Yizhang Lin,Sirui Hong,Duyi Pan,Yaying Fei,Guanghao Mei,Bangbang Liu,Tianqi Pang,Jacky Kwok,Ceyao Zhang,Bang Liu,Chenglin Wu
关键词-EN: Automated Machine Learning, approaches encompass traditional, Automated Machine, autonomously build pipelines, Machine Learning
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Software Engineering (cs.SE)
备注: The code is available at this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) approaches encompass traditional methods that optimize fixed pipelines for model selection and ensembling, as well as newer LLM-based frameworks that autonomously build pipelines. While LLM-based agents have shown promise in automating machine learning tasks, they often generate low-diversity and suboptimal code, even after multiple iterations. To overcome these limitations, we introduce Tree-Search Enhanced LLM Agents (SELA), an innovative agent-based system that leverages Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to optimize the AutoML process. By representing pipeline configurations as trees, our framework enables agents to conduct experiments intelligently and iteratively refine their strategies, facilitating a more effective exploration of the machine learning solution space. This novel approach allows SELA to discover optimal pathways based on experimental feedback, improving the overall quality of the solutions. In an extensive evaluation across 20 machine learning datasets, we compare the performance of traditional and agent-based AutoML methods, demonstrating that SELA achieves a win rate of 65% to 80% against each baseline across all datasets. These results underscore the significant potential of agent-based strategies in AutoML, offering a fresh perspective on tackling complex machine learning challenges.
摘要：自动化机器学习 (AutoML) 方法包括传统方法，这些方法针对模型选择和集成优化固定管道，以及基于大语言模型 (LLM) 的新框架，这些框架能够自主构建管道。尽管基于 LLM 的智能体在自动化机器学习任务方面显示出潜力，但它们在多次迭代后往往生成多样性低且次优的代码。为了克服这些限制，我们引入了树搜索增强的 LLM 智能体 (SELA)，这是一种创新的基于智能体的系统，利用蒙特卡洛树搜索 (MCTS) 来优化 AutoML 过程。通过将管道配置表示为树，我们的框架使智能体能够智能地进行实验并迭代地改进其策略，从而更有效地探索机器学习解决方案空间。这种新颖的方法使 SELA 能够基于实验反馈发现最优路径，从而提高解决方案的整体质量。在跨越 20 个机器学习数据集的广泛评估中，我们比较了传统和基于智能体的 AutoML 方法的性能，结果表明 SELA 在所有数据集上对每个基线的胜率为 65% 至 80%。这些结果突显了基于智能体的策略在 AutoML 中的显著潜力，为应对复杂的机器学习挑战提供了新的视角。

[NLP-5] Large Language Models Empowered Personalized Web Agents

【速读】： 该论文试图解决现有基于大语言模型（LLM）的Web代理在处理个性化指令时，未能充分利用用户个性化数据（如用户档案和历史Web行为）的问题。解决方案的关键在于提出了一个名为Personalized User Memory-enhanced Alignment (PUMA)的框架，该框架通过任务特定的检索策略从记忆库中筛选相关历史Web行为，并基于这些行为对LLM进行微调和直接偏好优化，从而实现个性化指令理解和动作执行的精准对齐。这一方法显著提升了Web代理在个性化任务中的表现。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17236
作者: Hongru Cai,Yongqi Li,Wenjie Wang,Fengbin Zhu,Xiaoyu Shen,Wenjie Li,Tat-Seng Chua
关键词-EN: Web agents, Large Language Models, Web, Personalized Web Agent, personalized Web
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Information Retrieval (cs.IR)
备注: The code and data are available on the project website this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Web agents have emerged as a promising direction to automate Web task completion based on user instructions, significantly enhancing user experience. Recently, Web agents have evolved from traditional agents to Large Language Models (LLMs)-based Web agents. Despite their success, existing LLM-based Web agents overlook the importance of personalized data (e.g., user profiles and historical Web behaviors) in assisting the understanding of users’ personalized instructions and executing customized actions. To overcome the limitation, we first formulate the task of LLM-empowered personalized Web agents, which integrate personalized data and user instructions to personalize instruction comprehension and action execution. To address the absence of a comprehensive evaluation benchmark, we construct a Personalized Web Agent Benchmark (PersonalWAB), featuring user instructions, personalized user data, Web functions, and two evaluation paradigms across three personalized Web tasks. Moreover, we propose a Personalized User Memory-enhanced Alignment (PUMA) framework to adapt LLMs to the personalized Web agent task. PUMA utilizes a memory bank with a task-specific retrieval strategy to filter relevant historical Web behaviors. Based on the behaviors, PUMA then aligns LLMs for personalized action execution through fine-tuning and direct preference optimization. Extensive experiments validate the superiority of PUMA over existing Web agents on PersonalWAB.
摘要：Web 智能体作为一种基于用户指令自动化完成 Web 任务的有前途的方向，显著提升了用户体验。近期，Web 智能体已从传统智能体演变为基于大语言模型 (LLM) 的 Web 智能体。尽管取得了成功，现有的基于 LLM 的 Web 智能体忽视了个性化数据（例如，用户档案和历史 Web 行为）在辅助理解用户个性化指令和执行定制化操作中的重要性。为了克服这一局限，我们首先制定了 LLM 赋能的个性化 Web 智能体任务，该任务整合了个性化数据和用户指令，以个性化指令理解和操作执行为目标。为了解决缺乏全面评估基准的问题，我们构建了一个个性化 Web 智能体基准 (PersonalWAB)，该基准包括用户指令、个性化用户数据、Web 功能以及跨三个个性化 Web 任务的两种评估范式。此外，我们提出了一个个性化用户记忆增强对齐 (PUMA) 框架，以适应 LLM 到个性化 Web 智能体任务。PUMA 利用带有任务特定检索策略的记忆库来筛选相关历史 Web 行为。基于这些行为，PUMA 通过微调和对齐 LLM 以进行个性化操作执行。大量实验验证了 PUMA 在 PersonalWAB 上优于现有 Web 智能体。

[NLP-6] Fine-Tuning Large Language Models to Appropriately Abstain with Semantic Entropy NEURIPS

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大语言模型（LLMs）在生成文本时可能产生的幻觉问题，特别是在需要高度准确性的关键应用领域，如医学或法律。解决方案的关键在于提出了一种基于语义熵的微调方法，该方法通过模型内部的自我反省来衡量不确定性，无需依赖外部标签，从而在不需要真实标签的情况下有效地减少幻觉现象，并在短文本和长文本生成任务中均表现出色。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17234
作者: Benedict Aaron Tjandra,Muhammed Razzak,Jannik Kossen,Kunal Handa,Yarin Gal
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, Large Language, inaccurate text, generate plausible, plausible but inaccurate
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: Accepted to NeurIPS Safe Generative AI Workshop 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models (LLMs) are known to hallucinate, whereby they generate plausible but inaccurate text. This phenomenon poses significant risks in critical applications, such as medicine or law, necessitating robust hallucination mitigation strategies. While recent works have proposed fine-tuning methods to teach LLMs to abstain from answering questions beyond their knowledge or capabilities, these methods rely on the existence of ground-truth labels or are limited to short-form responses. To address these limitations, we propose fine-tuning using semantic entropy, an uncertainty measure derived from introspection into the model which does not require external labels. We demonstrate that our approach matches or outperforms models fine-tuned using prior work and achieves strong performance for both short and long-form generations on a range of datasets.
摘要：大语言模型 (LLMs) 以其产生看似合理但不准确的文本（即“幻觉”）而闻名。这种现象在医疗或法律等关键应用中带来了重大风险，因此需要强有力的幻觉缓解策略。尽管最近的研究提出了通过微调方法来教导 LLMs 避免回答超出其知识或能力范围的问题，但这些方法依赖于存在真实标签或仅限于短格式响应。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了使用语义熵进行微调的方法，这是一种从模型内省中得出的不确定性度量，不需要外部标签。我们证明，我们的方法与使用先前工作进行微调的模型相匹配或超越，并在一系列数据集上实现了短格式和长格式生成的强大性能。

[NLP-7] Dhoroni: Exploring Bengali Climate Change and Environmental Views with a Multi-Perspective News Dataset and Natural Language Processing

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决气候变化研究中存在的语言和资源不平衡问题，特别是针对孟加拉语（Bangla）在气候变化和自然语言处理（NLP）领域的研究不足。解决方案的关键在于引入了一个名为Dhoroni的新型孟加拉语气候变化和环境新闻数据集，包含2300篇经过标注的新闻文章，涵盖政治影响、科学统计数据、真实性、立场检测和利益相关者参与等多个视角。此外，论文还提出了BanglaBERT-Dhoroni系列模型，作为孟加拉语气候和环境意见检测的基准模型，通过微调该数据集来提升模型的性能。这一研究显著增强了孟加拉语气候话语的可访问性和分析能力，填补了孟加拉国等气候影响严重地区在气候变化研究中的关键沟通和研究空白。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17225
作者: Azmine Toushik Wasi,Wahid Faisal,Taj Ahmad,Abdur Rahman,Mst Rafia Islam
关键词-EN: critical challenges globally, disproportionately affecting low-income, poses critical challenges, affecting low-income countries, change poses critical
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computers and Society (cs.CY); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Applications (stat.AP)
备注: In Review

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Climate change poses critical challenges globally, disproportionately affecting low-income countries that often lack resources and linguistic representation on the international stage. Despite Bangladesh’s status as one of the most vulnerable nations to climate impacts, research gaps persist in Bengali-language studies related to climate change and NLP. To address this disparity, we introduce Dhoroni, a novel Bengali (Bangla) climate change and environmental news dataset, comprising a 2300 annotated Bangla news articles, offering multiple perspectives such as political influence, scientific/statistical data, authenticity, stance detection, and stakeholder involvement. Furthermore, we present an in-depth exploratory analysis of Dhoroni and introduce BanglaBERT-Dhoroni family, a novel baseline model family for climate and environmental opinion detection in Bangla, fine-tuned on our dataset. This research contributes significantly to enhancing accessibility and analysis of climate discourse in Bengali (Bangla), addressing crucial communication and research gaps in climate-impacted regions like Bangladesh with 180 million people.
摘要：气候变化在全球范围内带来了严峻的挑战，尤其对低收入国家造成了不成比例的影响，这些国家在国际舞台上往往缺乏资源和语言代表性。尽管孟加拉国是受气候影响最脆弱的国家之一，但在与气候变化和自然语言处理（NLP）相关的孟加拉语研究中仍存在研究空白。为了解决这一差距，我们引入了 Dhoroni，这是一个新颖的孟加拉语（Bangla）气候变化和环境新闻数据集，包含 2300 篇经过标注的孟加拉语新闻文章，提供了多重视角，如政治影响、科学/统计数据、真实性、立场检测和利益相关者参与。此外，我们对 Dhoroni 进行了深入的探索性分析，并引入了 BanglaBERT-Dhoroni 系列，这是一个用于孟加拉语气候和环境意见检测的新颖基线模型系列，基于我们的数据集进行了微调。本研究显著提升了孟加拉语（Bangla）气候话语的可访问性和分析能力，解决了孟加拉国等气候影响地区（拥有 1.8 亿人口）在气候沟通和研究方面的关键差距。

[NLP-8] Context-aware Prompt Tuning: Advancing In-Context Learning with Adversarial Methods

【速读】： 该论文试图解决在少样本学习场景下，传统微调方法容易过拟合的问题，同时提升模型对训练样本信息的利用效率。解决方案的关键在于引入Context-aware Prompt Tuning (CPT)，结合In-Context Learning (ICL)和Prompt Tuning (PT)的思想，通过迭代优化上下文嵌入来深入挖掘训练样本的潜在信息。具体来说，CPT在ICL的基础上，通过类似PT的方式精细调整上下文嵌入，同时借鉴对抗攻击的方法，基于上下文中的标签调整输入，并使用投影梯度下降算法保持嵌入接近原始值，从而在多个分类任务中实现更高的准确性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17222
作者: Tsachi Blau,Moshe Kimhi,Yonatan Belinkov,Alexander Bronstein,Chaim Baskin
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, Fine-tuning Large Language, Large Language, typically involves updating, Language Models
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) typically involves updating at least a few billions of parameters. A more parameter-efficient approach is Prompt Tuning (PT), which updates only a few learnable tokens, and differently, In-Context Learning (ICL) adapts the model to a new task by simply including examples in the input without any training. When applying optimization-based methods, such as fine-tuning and PT for few-shot learning, the model is specifically adapted to the small set of training examples, whereas ICL leaves the model unchanged. This distinction makes traditional learning methods more prone to overfitting; in contrast, ICL is less sensitive to the few-shot scenario. While ICL is not prone to overfitting, it does not fully extract the information that exists in the training examples. This work introduces Context-aware Prompt Tuning (CPT), a method inspired by ICL, PT, and adversarial attacks. We build on the ICL strategy of concatenating examples before the input, but we extend this by PT-like learning, refining the context embedding through iterative optimization to extract deeper insights from the training examples. We carefully modify specific context tokens, considering the unique structure of input and output formats. Inspired by adversarial attacks, we adjust the input based on the labels present in the context, focusing on minimizing, rather than maximizing, the loss. Moreover, we apply a projected gradient descent algorithm to keep token embeddings close to their original values, under the assumption that the user-provided data is inherently valuable. Our method has been shown to achieve superior accuracy across multiple classification tasks using various LLM models.
摘要：微调大语言模型 (LLM) 通常涉及更新至少数十亿个参数。一种更为参数高效的方法是提示调优 (Prompt Tuning, PT)，它仅更新少数可学习的 Token。与此不同，上下文学习 (In-Context Learning, ICL) 通过在输入中包含示例来适应模型到新任务，而无需任何训练。当应用基于优化的方法（如微调和 PT）进行少样本学习时，模型会针对少量训练样本进行特定调整，而 ICL 则保持模型不变。这种区别使得传统学习方法更容易过拟合；相比之下，ICL 对少样本场景的敏感性较低。尽管 ICL 不易过拟合，但它并未完全提取训练示例中的信息。本研究引入了上下文感知提示调优 (Context-aware Prompt Tuning, CPT)，该方法受 ICL、PT 和对抗攻击的启发。我们在 ICL 策略的基础上，通过类似 PT 的学习方式，通过迭代优化来精炼上下文嵌入，以从训练示例中提取更深层次的见解。我们仔细修改特定的上下文 Token，考虑输入和输出格式的独特结构。受对抗攻击的启发，我们根据上下文中的标签调整输入，专注于最小化而非最大化损失。此外，我们应用投影梯度下降算法，以假设用户提供的数据本身具有价值，保持 Token 嵌入接近其原始值。我们的方法已在使用多种 LLM 模型的多个分类任务中展现出优越的准确性。

[NLP-9] Creativity in AI: Progresses and Challenges

【速读】： 该论文试图解决人工智能（AI）在创造力方面的现状和挑战问题。解决方案的关键在于全面评估AI的创造力，不仅关注其生成内容的质量，还需考虑创造过程的多维度特性，如新颖性、原创性和连贯性。论文提出，未来的研究应借鉴认知科学和心理学的理论，以提升AI在创造性问题解决、抽象思维和组合性任务中的表现，并解决生成模型带来的版权和作者身份问题。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17218
作者: Mete Ismayilzada,Debjit Paul,Antoine Bosselut,Lonneke van der Plas
关键词-EN: surprising ideas, human cognition, ability to produce, widely studied, crucial aspect
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 44 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Creativity is the ability to produce novel, useful, and surprising ideas, and has been widely studied as a crucial aspect of human cognition. Machine creativity on the other hand has been a long-standing challenge. With the rise of advanced generative AI, there has been renewed interest and debate regarding AI’s creative capabilities. Therefore, it is imperative to revisit the state of creativity in AI and identify key progresses and remaining challenges. In this work, we survey leading works studying the creative capabilities of AI systems, focusing on creative problem-solving, linguistic, artistic, and scientific creativity. Our review suggests that while the latest AI models are largely capable of producing linguistically and artistically creative outputs such as poems, images, and musical pieces, they struggle with tasks that require creative problem-solving, abstract thinking and compositionality and their generations suffer from a lack of diversity, originality, long-range incoherence and hallucinations. We also discuss key questions concerning copyright and authorship issues with generative models. Furthermore, we highlight the need for a comprehensive evaluation of creativity that is process-driven and considers several dimensions of creativity. Finally, we propose future research directions to improve the creativity of AI outputs, drawing inspiration from cognitive science and psychology.
摘要：创造力是产生新颖、有用且令人惊讶的想法的能力，长期以来一直被广泛研究为人脑认知的关键方面。机器创造力则一直是一个长期存在的挑战。随着先进生成式 AI (Generative AI) 的兴起，关于 AI 创造能力的兴趣和争论再次升温。因此，重新审视 AI 中的创造力现状，识别关键进展和剩余挑战显得尤为重要。在本研究中，我们调查了研究 AI 系统创造能力的领先工作，重点关注创造性问题解决、语言、艺术和科学创造力。我们的综述表明，尽管最新的 AI 模型在生成语言和艺术创意输出（如诗歌、图像和音乐作品）方面表现出色，但它们在需要创造性问题解决、抽象思维和组合性的任务上仍面临困难，其生成内容存在多样性、原创性、长程连贯性和幻觉缺失的问题。我们还讨论了与生成模型相关的版权和作者身份问题。此外，我们强调了需要对创造力进行全面评估，这种评估应基于过程驱动，并考虑创造力的多个维度。最后，我们提出了未来研究方向，以提升 AI 输出的创造力，借鉴认知科学和心理学中的灵感。

[NLP-10] MiniPLM: Knowledge Distillation for Pre-Training Language Models

【速读】： 该论文试图解决在预训练阶段进行知识蒸馏（Knowledge Distillation, KD）时面临的效率、灵活性和有效性问题。解决方案的关键在于提出了MiniPLM框架，通过离线教师语言模型（LM）推理来优化训练数据分布，从而在不增加训练时间成本的情况下，为多个学生LM提供知识蒸馏。MiniPLM不仅提高了训练数据的难度和多样性，还支持跨模型家族的蒸馏，显著提升了学生LM在下游任务中的表现和语言建模能力，同时减少了预训练的计算量。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17215
作者: Yuxian Gu,Hao Zhou,Fandong Meng,Jie Zhou,Minlie Huang
关键词-EN: LMs, high-performing student language, MiniPLM, student LMs, training data
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Knowledge distillation (KD) is widely used to train small, high-performing student language models (LMs) using large teacher LMs. While effective in fine-tuning, KD during pre-training faces challenges in efficiency, flexibility, and effectiveness. Existing methods either incur high computational costs due to online teacher inference, require tokenization matching between teacher and student LMs, or risk losing the difficulty and diversity of the teacher-generated training data. To address these issues, we propose MiniPLM, a KD framework for pre-training LMs by refining the training data distribution with the teacher’s knowledge. For efficiency, MiniPLM performs offline teacher LM inference, allowing KD for multiple student LMs without adding training-time costs. For flexibility, MiniPLM operates solely on the training corpus, enabling KD across model families. For effectiveness, MiniPLM leverages the differences between large and small LMs to enhance the difficulty and diversity of the training data, helping student LMs acquire versatile and sophisticated knowledge. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MiniPLM boosts the student LMs’ performance on 9 widely used downstream tasks, improves the language modeling capabilities, and reduces pre-training computation. The benefit of MiniPLM extends to large pre-training scales, evidenced by the extrapolation of the scaling curves. Further analysis reveals that MiniPLM supports KD across model families and enhances the utilization of pre-training data. Our model, code, and data are available at this https URL.
摘要：知识蒸馏 (Knowledge Distillation, KD) 广泛用于利用大型教师语言模型 (Language Models, LMs) 训练小型、高性能的学生语言模型。尽管在微调过程中有效，但在预训练阶段进行 KD 面临着效率、灵活性和有效性方面的挑战。现有方法要么由于在线教师推理而产生高计算成本，要么需要教师和学生 LMs 之间的 Tokenization 匹配，或者可能丢失教师生成训练数据的难度和多样性。为解决这些问题，我们提出了 MiniPLM，一种通过利用教师知识精炼训练数据分布来进行预训练 LMs 的 KD 框架。为提高效率，MiniPLM 执行离线教师 LM 推理，使得多个学生 LMs 的 KD 无需增加训练时间成本。为增强灵活性，MiniPLM 仅基于训练语料库运行，支持跨模型家族的 KD。为提升有效性，MiniPLM 利用大型和小型 LMs 之间的差异来增强训练数据的难度和多样性，帮助学生 LMs 获取多样化和复杂的知识。广泛的实验表明，MiniPLM 在 9 个广泛使用的下游任务上提升了学生 LMs 的性能，增强了语言建模能力，并减少了预训练计算量。MiniPLM 的益处扩展到大规模预训练，通过扩展缩放曲线得以证明。进一步分析显示，MiniPLM 支持跨模型家族的 KD，并增强了预训练数据的利用率。我们的模型、代码和数据可在 https URL 获取。

[NLP-11] Exploring Possibilities of AI-Powered Legal Assistance in Bangladesh through Large Language Modeling

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决孟加拉国法律体系中存在的延误、复杂性、高成本及大量未决案件等问题，这些问题导致许多人因缺乏知识或财务限制而放弃寻求法律帮助。解决方案的关键在于开发一个专门针对孟加拉国法律体系的大型语言模型（LLM），即GPT2-UKIL-EN。通过创建包含孟加拉国法律文档的英文语料库UKIL-DB-EN，并对GPT-2模型进行微调，该模型能够提供英语环境下的法律援助，并通过专家支持的案例研究进行严格评估，显示出其在孟加拉国法律事务中的潜在应用价值。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17210
作者: Azmine Toushik Wasi,Wahid Faisal,Mst Rafia Islam,Mahathir Mohammad Bappy
关键词-EN: Large Language Model, pursuing legal action, legal action due, specialized Large Language, legal system struggles
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computers and Society (cs.CY)
备注: In Review

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Purpose: Bangladesh’s legal system struggles with major challenges like delays, complexity, high costs, and millions of unresolved cases, which deter many from pursuing legal action due to lack of knowledge or financial constraints. This research seeks to develop a specialized Large Language Model (LLM) to assist in the Bangladeshi legal system. Methods: We created UKIL-DB-EN, an English corpus of Bangladeshi legal documents, by collecting and scraping data on various legal acts. We fine-tuned the GPT-2 model on this dataset to develop GPT2-UKIL-EN, an LLM focused on providing legal assistance in English. Results: The model was rigorously evaluated using semantic assessments, including case studies supported by expert opinions. The evaluation provided promising results, demonstrating the potential for the model to assist in legal matters within Bangladesh. Conclusion: Our work represents the first structured effort toward building an AI-based legal assistant for Bangladesh. While the results are encouraging, further refinements are necessary to improve the model’s accuracy, credibility, and safety. This is a significant step toward creating a legal AI capable of serving the needs of a population of 180 million.
摘要：
目的：孟加拉国的法律体系面临着重大挑战，如延迟、复杂性、高成本以及数百万未解决的案件，这些因素使得许多人因缺乏知识或财务限制而放弃追求法律行动。本研究旨在开发一种专门的大语言模型 (LLM) 以协助孟加拉国的法律体系。
方法：我们创建了 UKIL-DB-EN，一个包含孟加拉国法律文件的英文语料库，通过收集和抓取各种法律法案的数据。我们在此数据集上对 GPT-2 模型进行了微调，开发了 GPT2-UKIL-EN，这是一个专注于提供英文法律援助的 LLM。
结果：该模型通过包括专家意见支持的案例研究在内的语义评估进行了严格评估。评估结果显示了模型的潜力，表明其在孟加拉国法律事务中的辅助作用。
结论：我们的工作代表了为孟加拉国构建基于 AI 的法律助手的首个系统性努力。尽管结果令人鼓舞，但仍需进一步改进以提高模型的准确性、可信度和安全性。这是朝着创建能够服务于 1.8 亿人口需求的法律 AI 迈出的重要一步。

[NLP-12] Audio-to-Score Conversion Model Based on Whisper methodology

【速读】： 该论文试图解决从音乐音频中提取旋律和和弦并将其记录为ABC记谱法的问题。解决方案的关键在于开发了一个基于Whisper的Transformer模型，并创新性地引入了“Orpheus’ Score”自定义记谱系统，通过将音乐信息转换为tokens，设计了自定义词汇库和相应的tokenizer。此外，论文还定制了全面的数据处理流程，包括数据清洗、格式化和转换，并实施了变异机制以提高训练数据的多样性和质量。实验结果表明，该模型相较于传统算法在准确性和性能上有了显著提升。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17209
作者: Hongyao Zhang,Bohang Sun
关键词-EN: Transformer model based, based on Whisper, develops a Transformer, ABC notation, Transformer model
类目: ound (cs.SD); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: 5 pages, 7 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This thesis develops a Transformer model based on Whisper, which extracts melodies and chords from music audio and records them into ABC notation. A comprehensive data processing workflow is customized for ABC notation, including data cleansing, formatting, and conversion, and a mutation mechanism is implemented to increase the diversity and quality of training data. This thesis innovatively introduces the “Orpheus’ Score”, a custom notation system that converts music information into tokens, designs a custom vocabulary library, and trains a corresponding custom tokenizer. Experiments show that compared to traditional algorithms, the model has significantly improved accuracy and performance. While providing a convenient audio-to-score tool for music enthusiasts, this work also provides new ideas and tools for research in music information processing.
摘要：本论文基于 Whisper 开发了一个 Transformer 模型，该模型能够从音乐音频中提取旋律和和弦，并将其记录为 ABC 记谱法。为 ABC 记谱法定制了一个全面的数据处理工作流程，包括数据清洗、格式化和转换，并实现了一个变异机制以增加训练数据的多样性和质量。本论文创新性地引入了“Orpheus’ Score”，这是一种自定义的记谱系统，将音乐信息转换为 Token，设计了一个自定义词汇库，并训练了相应的自定义 Tokenizer。实验表明，与传统算法相比，该模型在准确性和性能上有了显著提升。这项工作不仅为音乐爱好者提供了一个便捷的音频转乐谱工具，也为音乐信息处理领域的研究提供了新的思路和工具。

[NLP-13] VoiceBench: Benchmarking LLM-Based Voice Assistants

【速读】： 该论文试图解决当前大型语言模型（LLM）语音助手缺乏全面评估基准的问题。解决方案的关键在于引入了VoiceBench，这是首个专门设计用于多维度评估LLM语音助手的基准。VoiceBench不仅包括真实的语音指令，还生成了合成语音指令，以模拟现实世界中多样化的说话者特征、环境和内容因素。通过广泛的实验，VoiceBench揭示了现有LLM语音助手模型的局限性，并为未来的研究和开发提供了宝贵的见解。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17196
作者: Yiming Chen,Xianghu Yue,Chen Zhang,Xiaoxue Gao,Robby T. Tan,Haizhou Li
关键词-EN: significantly improved user, improved user experience, user experience compared, LLM-based voice assistants, traditional text-based interactions
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Sound (cs.SD); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: Work in progress. Data is available at this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Building on the success of large language models (LLMs), recent advancements such as GPT-4o have enabled real-time speech interactions through LLM-based voice assistants, offering a significantly improved user experience compared to traditional text-based interactions. However, the absence of benchmarks designed to evaluate these speech interaction capabilities has hindered progress of LLM-based voice assistants development. Current evaluations focus primarily on automatic speech recognition (ASR) or general knowledge evaluation with clean speeches, neglecting the more intricate, real-world scenarios that involve diverse speaker characteristics, environmental and content factors. To address this, we introduce VoiceBench, the first benchmark designed to provide a multi-faceted evaluation of LLM-based voice assistants. VoiceBench also includes both real and synthetic spoken instructions that incorporate the above three key real-world variations. Extensive experiments reveal the limitations of current LLM-based voice assistant models and offer valuable insights for future research and development in this field.
摘要：基于大语言模型 (LLM) 的成功，如 GPT-4o 等最新进展已通过基于 LLM 的语音助手实现了实时语音交互，相较于传统的基于文本的交互，提供了显著提升的用户体验。然而，缺乏专门用于评估这些语音交互能力的基准，阻碍了基于 LLM 的语音助手的发展。当前的评估主要集中在自动语音识别 (ASR) 或通过清晰语音进行的一般知识评估，忽视了涉及多样说话者特征、环境和内容因素的更为复杂、真实的场景。为此，我们引入了 VoiceBench，这是首个旨在对基于 LLM 的语音助手进行多方面评估的基准。VoiceBench 还包括了结合上述三种关键现实世界变量的真实和合成语音指令。广泛的实验揭示了当前基于 LLM 的语音助手模型的局限性，并为该领域的未来研究和开发提供了宝贵的见解。

[NLP-14] Language Model Non-myopic Generation for Reasoning and Planning

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大型语言模型（LLMs）在推理和规划过程中由于自回归解码的短视特性导致的规划不准确和非最优问题。解决方案的关键在于提出了一种名为“预测解码”（Predictive-Decoding）的新方法，该方法利用模型预测控制（Model Predictive Control）技术，通过重新加权LLM的分布以考虑未来的轨迹，从而减少早期错误并促进非短视的规划。实验结果表明，这种方法在数学、编码和代理任务中显著提高了规划的准确性和效率，同时减少了计算资源的消耗。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17195
作者: Chang Ma,Haiteng Zhao,Junlei Zhang,Junxian He,Lingpeng Kong
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, Large Language, demonstrated remarkable abilities, Language Models, Model Predictive Control
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models have demonstrated remarkable abilities in reasoning and planning by breaking down complex problems into sequential steps. Despite their success in various domains like mathematical problem-solving and coding, LLMs face challenges in ensuring reliable and optimal planning due to their inherent myopic nature of autoregressive decoding. This paper revisits LLM reasoning from an optimal-control perspective, proposing a novel method, Predictive-Decoding, that leverages Model Predictive Control to enhance planning accuracy. By re-weighting LLM distributions based on foresight trajectories, Predictive-Decoding aims to mitigate early errors and promote non-myopic planning. Our experiments show significant improvements in a wide range of tasks for math, coding, and agents. Furthermore, Predictive-Decoding demonstrates computational efficiency, outperforming search baselines with reduced computational resources. This study provides insights into optimizing LLM planning capabilities.
摘要：大语言模型通过将复杂问题分解为连续步骤，展示了在推理和规划方面的显著能力。尽管在数学问题解决和编码等领域取得了成功，但由于自回归解码的固有短视特性，大语言模型在确保可靠和最优规划方面仍面临挑战。本文从最优控制的角度重新审视大语言模型的推理，提出了一种新方法——预测解码 (Predictive-Decoding)，该方法利用模型预测控制来提高规划的准确性。通过根据前瞻轨迹重新加权大语言模型的分布，预测解码旨在减少早期错误并促进非短视规划。我们的实验显示，在数学、编码和智能体等多个任务中，预测解码显著提升了性能。此外，预测解码还展示了计算效率，在减少计算资源的情况下优于搜索基线。本研究为优化大语言模型的规划能力提供了见解。

[NLP-15] From Attention to Activation: Unravelling the Enigmas of Large Language Models

【速读】： 该论文试图解决自回归Transformer模型中的两个异常现象：（1）注意力头对第一个token的过度关注；（2）隐藏状态中出现的大量异常激活值。解决方案的关键在于：首先，通过将softmax函数重新表述为softmax-1来减少对第一个token的关注；其次，通过引入OrthoAdam优化器，利用正交矩阵转换梯度，以解决由自适应优化器（如Adam）引起的异常激活问题。这些方法不仅有效缓解了上述现象，还使得Transformer模型在4位权重量化下仍能保持性能，而标准方法无法实现这一点。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17174
作者: Prannay Kaul,Chengcheng Ma,Ismail Elezi,Jiankang Deng
关键词-EN: large outlier activations, attention heads, study two strange, auto-regressive Transformers, large outlier
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 10 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We study two strange phenomena in auto-regressive Transformers: (1) the dominance of the first token in attention heads; (2) the occurrence of large outlier activations in the hidden states. We find that popular large language models, such as Llama attend maximally to the first token in 98% of attention heads, a behaviour we attribute to the softmax function. To mitigate this issue, we propose a reformulation of softmax to softmax-1. Furthermore, we identify adaptive optimisers, e.g. Adam, as the primary contributor to the large outlier activations and introduce OrthoAdam, a novel optimiser that utilises orthogonal matrices to transform gradients, to address this issue. Finally, not only do our methods prevent these phenomena from occurring, but additionally, they enable Transformers to sustain their performance when quantised using basic algorithms, something that standard methods are unable to do. In summary, our methods reduce the attention proportion on the first token from 65% to 3.3%, the activation kurtosis in the hidden states from 1657 to 3.1, and perplexity penalty under 4-bit weight quantisation from 3565 to 0.3.
摘要：我们研究了自回归 Transformer 中的两个奇特现象：(1) 注意力头对第一个 Token 的主导性；(2) 隐藏状态中出现的大量异常激活。我们发现，如 Llama 这样的流行大语言模型在 98% 的注意力头中最大程度地关注第一个 Token，这一行为我们归因于 softmax 函数。为了缓解这一问题，我们提出了一种 softmax 的重新表述，即 softmax-1。此外，我们识别出自适应优化器（如 Adam）是大量异常激活的主要贡献者，并引入了 OrthoAdam，一种利用正交矩阵变换梯度的新型优化器，以解决这一问题。最后，我们的方法不仅防止了这些现象的发生，而且还使得 Transformer 在使用基本算法量化时能够保持其性能，而标准方法无法做到这一点。总之，我们的方法将第一个 Token 的注意力比例从 65% 降低到 3.3%，隐藏状态中的激活峰度从 1657 降低到 3.1，并且在 4 位权重量化下的困惑度惩罚从 3565 降低到 0.3。

[NLP-16] Self-calibration for Language Model Quantization and Pruning

【速读】： 该论文试图解决模型压缩中的量化和剪枝方法在训练后需要校准数据的问题，特别是校准数据可能不具代表性以及组织机构不愿公开训练数据的问题。解决方案的关键是提出了一种自校准（self-calibration）方法，该方法无需外部数据，而是利用模型自身生成合成校准数据，以更好地近似预训练数据分布，从而在各种模型、压缩方法和任务中实现更优的下游任务性能。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17170
作者: Miles Williams,George Chrysostomou,Nikolaos Aletras
关键词-EN: enabling efficient inference, Quantization and pruning, enabling efficient, fundamental approaches, efficient inference
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Work in progress

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Quantization and pruning are fundamental approaches for model compression, enabling efficient inference for language models. In a post-training setting, state-of-the-art quantization and pruning methods require calibration data, a small set of unlabeled examples. Conventionally, randomly sampled web text is used, aiming to reflect the model training data. However, this poses two key problems: (1) unrepresentative calibration examples can harm model performance, and (2) organizations increasingly avoid releasing model training data. In this paper, we propose self-calibration as a solution. Our approach requires no external data, instead leveraging the model itself to generate synthetic calibration data as a better approximation of the pre-training data distribution. We extensively compare the performance of self-calibration with several baselines, across a variety of models, compression methods, and tasks. Our approach proves consistently competitive in maximizing downstream task performance, frequently outperforming even using real data.
摘要：量化和剪枝是模型压缩的基本方法，能够为语言模型实现高效的推理。在训练后阶段，最先进的量化和剪枝方法需要校准数据，即一小部分未标记的样本。传统上，随机抽样的网络文本被用作校准数据，旨在反映模型的训练数据。然而，这带来了两个关键问题：(1) 不具代表性的校准样本可能损害模型性能；(2) 组织机构越来越避免公开模型训练数据。本文提出自校准作为解决方案。我们的方法无需外部数据，而是利用模型自身生成合成校准数据，以更好地近似预训练数据分布。我们广泛比较了自校准与多个基线方法在多种模型、压缩方法和任务中的性能。我们的方法在最大化下游任务性能方面表现出色，经常优于使用真实数据的效果。

[NLP-17] Interchangeable Token Embeddings for Extendable Vocabulary and Alpha-Equivalence

【速读】： 该论文试图解决在语言模型中学习可互换的词汇标记以扩展词汇表并泛化到新标记的问题。解决方案的关键在于提出了一种双部分嵌入方法：第一部分是所有可互换标记共享的，确保它们代表相同的概念核心；第二部分是为每个标记随机生成的，以确保它们的可区分性。这种方法不仅增强了模型的泛化能力，还引入了对alpha-等价性的有利归纳偏置。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17161
作者: İlker Işık,Ramazan Gokberk Cinbis,Ebru Aydin Gol
关键词-EN: learning interchangeable tokens, learning interchangeable, Abstract, tokens, expression preserves semantics
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Logic in Computer Science (cs.LO)
备注: 14 pages, 5 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We propose a novel approach for learning interchangeable tokens in language models to obtain an extendable vocabulary that can generalize to new tokens. Our method is designed to address alpha-equivalence, the principle that renaming bound variables in a syntactic expression preserves semantics. This property arises in many formal languages such as temporal logics, in which all proposition symbols represent the same concept but are distinguishable from each other. To handle such tokens, we develop a dual-part embedding approach. The first part is shared across all interchangeable tokens, thereby enforcing that they represent the same core concept. The second part is randomly generated for each token, which enables distinguishability. We evaluate our method in a Transformer encoder-decoder model on two tasks: solving linear temporal logic formulae and copying with extendable vocabulary. Our method demonstrates promising generalization capabilities in addition to introducing a favorable inductive bias for alpha-equivalence.
摘要：我们提出了一种新颖的方法，用于在语言模型中学习可互换的 Token，以获得一个可扩展的词汇表，该词汇表能够泛化到新的 Token。我们的方法旨在解决 alpha-等价性问题，即在句法表达式中重命名绑定变量会保留语义的原则。这一特性在许多形式语言中出现，例如时序逻辑，其中所有命题符号代表相同的概念，但彼此之间可区分。为了处理此类 Token，我们开发了一种双部分嵌入方法。第一部分在所有可互换的 Token 之间共享，从而强制它们代表相同的核心概念。第二部分为每个 Token 随机生成，从而实现可区分性。我们在 Transformer 编码器-解码器模型中评估了我们的方法，涉及两个任务：解决线性时序逻辑公式和使用可扩展词汇表进行复制。我们的方法不仅展示了有前景的泛化能力，还为 alpha-等价性引入了有利的归纳偏置。

[NLP-18] Improving Pinterest Search Relevance Using Large Language Models CIKM2024

【速读】： 该论文旨在提升Pinterest搜索的相关性评分，关键解决方案是将大型语言模型（LLMs）整合到搜索相关性模型中，通过精心设计的文本表示来有效预测Pin的相关性。具体方法包括利用搜索查询、生成视觉语言模型提取的标题、链接文本数据、高质量历史查询、用户自定义板块、Pin标题和描述等多源信息，构建鲁棒的预测模型。此外，采用半监督学习方法扩展训练数据，并利用多语言LLMs处理非英语数据，最终通过模型蒸馏技术将LLM模型转化为实时可用的模型架构和特征。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17152
作者: Han Wang,Mukuntha Narayanan Sundararaman,Onur Gungor,Yu Xu,Krishna Kamath,Rakesh Chalasani,Kurchi Subhra Hazra,Jinfeng Rao
关键词-EN: integrate Large Language, leveraging carefully designed, Large Language Models, scoring on Pinterest, integrate Large
类目: Information Retrieval (cs.IR); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: CIKM 2024 Workshop on Industrial Recommendation Systems

点击查看摘要

Abstract:To improve relevance scoring on Pinterest Search, we integrate Large Language Models (LLMs) into our search relevance model, leveraging carefully designed text representations to predict the relevance of Pins effectively. Our approach uses search queries alongside content representations that include captions extracted from a generative visual language model. These are further enriched with link-based text data, historically high-quality engaged queries, user-curated boards, Pin titles and Pin descriptions, creating robust models for predicting search relevance. We use a semi-supervised learning approach to efficiently scale up the amount of training data, expanding beyond the expensive human labeled data available. By utilizing multilingual LLMs, our system extends training data to include unseen languages and domains, despite initial data and annotator expertise being confined to English. Furthermore, we distill from the LLM-based model into real-time servable model architectures and features. We provide comprehensive offline experimental validation for our proposed techniques and demonstrate the gains achieved through the final deployed system at scale.
摘要：为了提升 Pinterest 搜索的相关性评分，我们将大语言模型 (LLM) 整合到我们的搜索相关性模型中，利用精心设计的文本表示来有效预测 Pin 的相关性。我们的方法结合了搜索查询与内容表示，这些内容表示包括从生成式视觉语言模型中提取的标题，并进一步丰富了基于链接的文本数据、历史高质量参与查询、用户精选的板块、Pin 标题和 Pin 描述，从而构建了强大的搜索相关性预测模型。我们采用半监督学习方法，以高效扩展训练数据量，超越了昂贵的人工标注数据的限制。通过利用多语言 LLM，我们的系统扩展了训练数据，涵盖了未见过的语言和领域，尽管初始数据和标注者专业知识仅限于英语。此外，我们将基于 LLM 的模型提炼为实时可用的模型架构和特征。我们为所提出的技术提供了全面的离线实验验证，并展示了最终部署系统在规模化应用中取得的成果。

[NLP-19] Can General-Purpose Large Language Models Generalize to English-Thai Machine Translation ? EMNLP2024

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大型语言模型（LLMs）在低资源和低计算环境下的泛化能力不足的问题。解决方案的关键在于强调专用模型的重要性，这些模型在资源受限的情况下，如4-bit量化约束下，能够持续优于LLMs，从而在资源受限的环境中保持高性能。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17145
作者: Jirat Chiaranaipanich,Naiyarat Hanmatheekuna,Jitkapat Sawatphol,Krittamate Tiankanon,Jiramet Kinchagawat,Amrest Chinkamol,Parinthapat Pengpun,Piyalitt Ittichaiwong,Peerat Limkonchotiwat
关键词-EN: Large language models, Large language, low-computation settings, common tasks, tasks but struggle
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: Accepted in GenBench EMNLP 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large language models (LLMs) perform well on common tasks but struggle with generalization in low-resource and low-computation settings. We examine this limitation by testing various LLMs and specialized translation models on English-Thai machine translation and code-switching datasets. Our findings reveal that under more strict computational constraints, such as 4-bit quantization, LLMs fail to translate effectively. In contrast, specialized models, with comparable or lower computational requirements, consistently outperform LLMs. This underscores the importance of specialized models for maintaining performance under resource constraints.
摘要：大语言模型 (LLMs) 在常见任务中表现出色，但在低资源和低计算环境下难以实现泛化。我们通过在英语-泰语机器翻译和代码转换数据集上测试各种 LLMs 和专用翻译模型来研究这一限制。我们的研究发现，在更严格的计算约束下，例如 4-bit 量化，LLMs 无法有效进行翻译。相比之下，专用模型在计算要求相当或更低的情况下，始终优于 LLMs。这突显了在资源受限条件下，专用模型对于保持性能的重要性。

[NLP-20] Aligning Large Language Models via Self-Steering Optimization

【速读】： 该论文试图解决自动化对齐系统中缺乏高质量偏好信号的问题，关键解决方案是引入自引导优化（Self-Steering Optimization, SSO）算法。SSO通过在迭代训练过程中基于预定义原则自主生成高质量的偏好信号，无需人工标注，从而消除了对手动注释的依赖。该算法通过确保所选和拒绝的响应之间保持一致的差距，并使它们都符合当前策略模型的学习能力，从而维持信号的准确性。SSO不仅适用于策略模型的在线和离线训练，还能增强奖励模型的训练效果。实验结果表明，SSO在迭代训练中提供了准确且符合策略的偏好信号，显著提升了模型在多个基准测试中的性能，并显著增强了奖励模型的表现。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17131
作者: Hao Xiang,Bowen Yu,Hongyu Lin,Keming Lu,Yaojie Lu,Xianpei Han,Le Sun,Jingren Zhou,Junyang Lin
关键词-EN: minimal human intervention, develops alignment systems, Automated alignment develops, alignment develops alignment, SSO
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Automated alignment develops alignment systems with minimal human intervention. The key to automated alignment lies in providing learnable and accurate preference signals for preference learning without human annotation. In this paper, we introduce Self-Steering Optimization ( SSO ), an algorithm that autonomously generates high-quality preference signals based on predefined principles during iterative training, eliminating the need for manual annotation. SSO maintains the accuracy of signals by ensuring a consistent gap between chosen and rejected responses while keeping them both on-policy to suit the current policy model’s learning capacity. SSO can benefit the online and offline training of the policy model, as well as enhance the training of reward models. We validate the effectiveness of SSO with two foundation models, Qwen2 and Llama3.1, indicating that it provides accurate, on-policy preference signals throughout iterative training. Without any manual annotation or external models, SSO leads to significant performance improvements across six subjective or objective benchmarks. Besides, the preference data generated by SSO significantly enhanced the performance of the reward model on Rewardbench. Our work presents a scalable approach to preference optimization, paving the way for more efficient and effective automated alignment.
摘要：自动化对齐通过最小化人工干预来开发对齐系统。自动化对齐的关键在于为偏好学习提供可学习的、准确的偏好信号，而无需人工标注。本文介绍了一种名为自引导优化 (Self-Steering Optimization, SSO) 的算法，该算法在迭代训练过程中基于预定义原则自主生成高质量的偏好信号，从而消除了手动标注的需求。SSO 通过确保所选和拒绝的响应之间保持一致的差距，同时使两者都符合当前策略模型的学习能力，从而保持信号的准确性。SSO 不仅有利于策略模型的在线和离线训练，还能增强奖励模型的训练效果。我们通过两个基础模型 Qwen2 和 Llama3.1 验证了 SSO 的有效性，表明其在迭代训练过程中始终提供准确、符合策略的偏好信号。在没有人工标注或外部模型的情况下，SSO 在六个主观或客观基准测试中显著提升了性能。此外，SSO 生成的偏好数据显著提升了奖励模型在 Rewardbench 上的表现。我们的工作提出了一种可扩展的偏好优化方法，为更高效、更有效的自动化对齐铺平了道路。

[NLP-21] PAPILLON: PrivAcy Preservation from Internet-based and Local Language MOdel ENsembles

【速读】： 该论文试图解决用户在使用大型语言模型（LLM）时可能泄露敏感信息的问题。解决方案的关键在于提出了一种名为“隐私意识委托”（Privacy-Conscious Delegation）的新任务，通过将API驱动的模型与本地托管的模型相结合，形成一个多阶段的LLM管道（PAPILLON）。该管道利用提示优化技术，在保持高响应质量的同时，将隐私泄露限制在7.5%以内，从而在保护用户隐私的同时，尽可能接近专有前沿模型的生成质量。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17127
作者: Li Siyan,Vethavikashini Chithrra Raghuram,Omar Khattab,Julia Hirschberg,Zhou Yu
关键词-EN: raising significant privacy, divulge sensitive information, proprietary LLM providers, significant privacy concerns, raising significant
类目: Cryptography and Security (cs.CR); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Users can divulge sensitive information to proprietary LLM providers, raising significant privacy concerns. While open-source models, hosted locally on the user’s machine, alleviate some concerns, models that users can host locally are often less capable than proprietary frontier models. Toward preserving user privacy while retaining the best quality, we propose Privacy-Conscious Delegation, a novel task for chaining API-based and local models. We utilize recent public collections of user-LLM interactions to construct a natural benchmark called PUPA, which contains personally identifiable information (PII). To study potential approaches, we devise PAPILLON, a multi-stage LLM pipeline that uses prompt optimization to address a simpler version of our task. Our best pipeline maintains high response quality for 85.5% of user queries while restricting privacy leakage to only 7.5%. We still leave a large margin to the generation quality of proprietary LLMs for future work. Our data and code will be available at this https URL.
摘要：用户可能会向专有的大语言模型 (LLM) 提供商泄露敏感信息，这引发了重大的隐私问题。虽然开源模型在用户本地机器上运行可以缓解一些担忧，但用户能够本地托管的模型通常能力不如专有前沿模型。为了在保留最佳质量的同时保护用户隐私，我们提出了隐私意识委托 (Privacy-Conscious Delegation)，这是一种将基于 API 的模型和本地模型串联起来的新颖任务。我们利用最近公开的用户与 LLM 交互集合，构建了一个名为 PUPA 的自然基准，其中包含个人身份信息 (PII)。为了研究潜在的方法，我们设计了 PAPILLON，这是一个多阶段 LLM 流水线，使用提示优化来解决我们任务的简化版本。我们的最佳流水线在 85.5% 的用户查询中保持了高质量响应，同时将隐私泄露限制在仅 7.5%。我们仍为未来工作留下了较大的生成质量改进空间。我们的数据和代码将在此 https URL 上提供。

[NLP-22] Exploring RL-based LLM Training for Formal Language Tasks with Programmed Rewards

【速读】： 该论文试图解决直接使用编程奖励信号进行强化学习（RL）训练大型语言模型（LLMs）的可行性问题。解决方案的关键在于通过编程定义明确的奖励函数，直接评估生成输出的质量，并应用于形式语言任务（如数学和编程）。论文提出了一种新的批量熵正则化项来辅助探索，以应对纯RL训练在复杂任务中的挑战，尽管训练稳定性尚未完全解决。研究结果表明，直接RL训练可能更适合于模型对齐等较小调整，而非完全学习新任务。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17126
作者: Alexander G. Padula,Dennis J.N.J. Soemers
关键词-EN: Proximal Policy Optimization, Proximal Policy, Policy Optimization, Human Feedback, Reinforcement Learning
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: Accepted at BNAIC 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) is commonly used in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback to align large language models (LLMs) with downstream tasks. This paper investigates the feasibility of using PPO for direct reinforcement learning (RL) from explicitly programmed reward signals, as opposed to indirect learning from human feedback via an intermediary reward model. We focus on tasks expressed through formal languages, such as mathematics and programming, where explicit reward functions can be programmed to automatically assess the quality of generated outputs. We apply this approach to a sentiment alignment task, a simple arithmetic task, and a more complex game synthesis task. The sentiment alignment task replicates prior research and serves to validate our experimental setup. Our results show that pure RL-based training for the two formal language tasks is challenging, with success being limited even for the simple arithmetic task. We propose a novel batch-entropy regularization term to aid exploration, although training is not yet entirely stable. Our findings suggest that direct RL training of LLMs may be more suitable for relatively minor changes, such as alignment, than for learning new tasks altogether, even if an informative reward signal can be expressed programmatically.
摘要：近端策略优化 (Proximal Policy Optimization, PPO) 在从人类反馈中进行强化学习以对齐大语言模型 (Large Language Models, LLMs) 与下游任务方面被广泛使用。本文探讨了使用 PPO 进行直接强化学习 (Reinforcement Learning, RL) 的可行性，即通过显式编程的奖励信号而非通过中介奖励模型间接从人类反馈中学习。我们专注于通过形式语言表达的任务，如数学和编程，在这些任务中可以编程显式奖励函数来自动评估生成输出的质量。我们将此方法应用于情感对齐任务、简单的算术任务以及更复杂的游戏合成任务。情感对齐任务复制了先前的研究，并用于验证我们的实验设置。我们的结果表明，基于纯 RL 的训练对于这两个形式语言任务具有挑战性，即使对于简单的算术任务，成功也是有限的。我们提出了一种新颖的批量熵正则化项来辅助探索，尽管训练尚未完全稳定。我们的研究结果表明，直接对 LLMs 进行 RL 训练可能更适合于相对较小的改变，如对齐，而不是完全学习新任务，即使可以编程表达信息丰富的奖励信号。

[NLP-23] Enhancing Answer Attribution for Faithful Text Generation with Large Language Models

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大语言模型（LLMs）在生成答案时如何进行有效归因的问题。解决方案的关键在于提出新的方法来生成更独立和上下文相关的声明，以改进答案分割和证据检索的子任务，从而提升答案归因的性能。通过评估，这些新方法被证明能够提高答案归因组件的性能。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17112
作者: Juraj Vladika,Luca Mülln,Florian Matthes
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, Language Models, Large Language, AI-based conversational systems, popularity of Large
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Information Retrieval (cs.IR)
备注: Accepted to KDIR 2024 (part of IC3K 2024)

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The increasing popularity of Large Language Models (LLMs) in recent years has changed the way users interact with and pose questions to AI-based conversational systems. An essential aspect for increasing the trustworthiness of generated LLM answers is the ability to trace the individual claims from responses back to relevant sources that support them, the process known as answer attribution. While recent work has started exploring the task of answer attribution in LLMs, some challenges still remain. In this work, we first perform a case study analyzing the effectiveness of existing answer attribution methods, with a focus on subtasks of answer segmentation and evidence retrieval. Based on the observed shortcomings, we propose new methods for producing more independent and contextualized claims for better retrieval and attribution. The new methods are evaluated and shown to improve the performance of answer attribution components. We end with a discussion and outline of future directions for the task.
摘要：近年来，大语言模型 (LLM) 的日益普及改变了用户与基于 AI 的对话系统互动和提问的方式。提高生成 LLM 答案的可信度的一个关键方面是能够将响应中的个别声明追溯到支持它们的相关来源，这一过程被称为答案归属 (answer attribution)。尽管最近的研究已经开始探索 LLM 中的答案归属任务，但仍存在一些挑战。在本研究中，我们首先进行了一项案例研究，分析了现有答案归属方法的有效性，重点考察了答案分段和证据检索这两个子任务。基于观察到的不足，我们提出了新的方法，以生成更独立和上下文关联的声明，从而提高检索和归属的效果。新方法经过评估，显示其能够提升答案归属组件的性能。最后，我们对任务进行了讨论，并概述了未来的研究方向。

[NLP-24] Human-LLM Hybrid Text Answer Aggregation for Crowd Annotations EMNLP2024

【速读】： 该论文试图解决众包标注中封闭式文本答案的聚合问题，特别是探讨大型语言模型（LLMs）作为聚合器的潜力。解决方案的关键在于提出了一种人机混合的文本答案聚合方法，即Creator-Aggregator Multi-Stage (CAMS)众包框架，通过多阶段的众包工作与LLMs的协作，实现了更有效的答案聚合。实验结果表明，这种结合众包工作者与LLMs的方法在公共众包数据集上的表现显著优于传统方法。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17099
作者: Jiyi Li
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, crucial issue, text answer aggregation, Answer aggregation, crowd
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: Accepted in EMNLP 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The quality is a crucial issue for crowd annotations. Answer aggregation is an important type of solution. The aggregated answers estimated from multiple crowd answers to the same instance are the eventually collected annotations, rather than the individual crowd answers themselves. Recently, the capability of Large Language Models (LLMs) on data annotation tasks has attracted interest from researchers. Most of the existing studies mainly focus on the average performance of individual crowd workers; several recent works studied the scenarios of aggregation on categorical labels and LLMs used as label creators. However, the scenario of aggregation on text answers and the role of LLMs as aggregators are not yet well-studied. In this paper, we investigate the capability of LLMs as aggregators in the scenario of close-ended crowd text answer aggregation. We propose a human-LLM hybrid text answer aggregation method with a Creator-Aggregator Multi-Stage (CAMS) crowdsourcing framework. We make the experiments based on public crowdsourcing datasets. The results show the effectiveness of our approach based on the collaboration of crowd workers and LLMs.
摘要：众包标注的质量是一个关键问题。答案聚合是解决这一问题的重要方法之一。通过聚合多个众包答案得到的估计值，最终被收集为标注结果，而非单个众包答案本身。近年来，大语言模型 (LLM) 在数据标注任务中的能力引起了研究者的兴趣。现有的大多数研究主要集中在个体众包工作者的平均表现上；一些近期的工作研究了在分类标签上的聚合场景以及 LLM 作为标签创建者的情况。然而，在文本答案上的聚合场景以及 LLM 作为聚合者的角色尚未得到充分研究。本文探讨了在封闭式众包文本答案聚合场景中，LLM 作为聚合者的能力。我们提出了一种人机混合的文本答案聚合方法，采用 Creator-Aggregator 多阶段 (CAMS) 众包框架。我们在公开的众包数据集上进行了实验。结果显示，基于众包工作者和 LLM 协作的方法具有显著的有效性。

[NLP-25] am Ryus Submission to SIGMORPHON 2024 Shared Task on Subword Tokenization

【速读】： 该论文试图解决的问题是如何将形态分割方法应用于子词分词器中，并探讨其有效性。解决方案的关键在于采用了两种方法：统计分割方法Morfessor和基于Transformer的序列到序列（seq2seq）分割模型。研究结果表明，形态分割方法在子词分词器中同样有效，并且通过保持高频词为独特标记，可以实现平衡的词汇频率分布，从而提升语言模型的性能。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17094
作者: Zilong Li
关键词-EN: canceled SIGMORPHON, team Ryu, shared task, papers presents, SIGMORPHON
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This papers presents the submission of team Ryu to the canceled SIGMORPHON 2024 shared task on subword tokenization. My submission explores whether morphological segmentation methods can be used as a part of subword tokenizers. I adopt two approaches: the statistical segmentation method Morfessor and a transformer based sequence-to-sequence (seq2seq) segmentation model in tokenizers. The prediction results show that morphological segmentation could be as effective as commonly used subword tokenizers. Additionally, I investigate how a tokenizer’s vocabulary influences the performance of language models. A tokenizer with a balanced token frequency distribution tends to work better. A balanced token vocabulary can be achieved by keeping frequent words as unique tokens.
摘要：本文提交了团队 Ryu 参与被取消的 SIGMORPHON 2024 共享任务中关于子词 Tokenization 的研究。本研究探讨了形态分割方法是否可以作为子词 Tokenizer 的一部分。采用了两种方法：统计分割方法 Morfessor 和基于 Transformer 的序列到序列 (seq2seq) 分割模型。预测结果表明，形态分割可以与常用的子词 Tokenizer 一样有效。此外，还研究了 Tokenizer 的词汇如何影响语言模型的性能。具有平衡 Token 频率分布的 Tokenizer 往往表现更好。通过将高频词保留为唯一 Token，可以实现平衡的 Token 词汇。

[NLP-26] Science Out of Its Ivory Tower: Improving Accessibility with Reinforcement Learning

【速读】： 该论文试图解决学术文献因专业术语和复杂语言导致公众难以理解的问题。解决方案的关键在于引入一个强化学习框架，通过微调语言模型，使其能够将学术摘要重写为更易理解的形式。该框架通过平衡词级和句子级的可读性奖励，有效替代专业术语为更通俗的表达，相较于传统监督微调或基于常规可读性指标的方法，显著提高了摘要的可读性，同时保持了事实准确性和语言质量。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17088
作者: Haining Wang,Jason Clark,Hannah McKelvey,Leila Sterman,Zheng Gao,Zuoyu Tian,Sandra Kübler,Xiaozhong Liu
关键词-EN: published daily, vast amount, remains inaccessible, due to dense, dense jargon
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computers and Society (cs.CY)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:A vast amount of scholarly work is published daily, yet much of it remains inaccessible to the general public due to dense jargon and complex language. To address this challenge in science communication, we introduce a reinforcement learning framework that fine-tunes a language model to rewrite scholarly abstracts into more comprehensible versions. Guided by a carefully balanced combination of word- and sentence-level accessibility rewards, our language model effectively substitutes technical terms with more accessible alternatives, a task which models supervised fine-tuned or guided by conventional readability measures struggle to accomplish. Our best model adjusts the readability level of scholarly abstracts by approximately six U.S. grade levels – in other words, from a postgraduate to a high school level. This translates to roughly a 90% relative boost over the supervised fine-tuning baseline, all while maintaining factual accuracy and high-quality language. An in-depth analysis of our approach shows that balanced rewards lead to systematic modifications in the base model, likely contributing to smoother optimization and superior performance. We envision this work as a step toward bridging the gap between scholarly research and the general public, particularly younger readers and those without a college degree.
摘要： 每天都有大量的学术成果发表，但由于专业术语和复杂语言的使用，许多内容对普通公众来说仍然难以理解。为了解决科学传播中的这一挑战，我们引入了一个强化学习框架，该框架对语言模型进行微调，使其能够将学术摘要改写为更易理解的形式。通过精心平衡的词级和句子级可读性奖励的引导，我们的语言模型能够有效地将专业术语替换为更易理解的替代词，而这一任务对于那些通过传统可读性指标进行监督微调或引导的模型来说，是难以完成的。我们的最佳模型将学术摘要的可读性水平调整了大约六个美国年级水平——换句话说，从研究生水平调整到高中水平。这相对于监督微调基线而言，大约提升了90%的相对效果，同时保持了事实准确性和高质量的语言表达。对我们的方法进行的深入分析表明，平衡的奖励机制导致了基础模型的系统性修改，这可能有助于更平滑的优化过程和更优异的性能表现。我们设想这项工作是弥合学术研究与普通公众之间差距的一步，特别是对于年轻读者和没有大学学位的人群。

[NLP-27] Continuous Speech Tokenizer in Text To Speech

【速读】： 该论文试图解决离散语音标记器在文本到语音任务中存在的信息损失问题。解决方案的关键在于提出了一种简单而有效的连续语音标记器，并基于此构建了文本到语音模型。通过连续语音标记器，模型在频率域的低频和高频段均能更好地保留信息，从而提高了语音的连续性和估计的平均意见得分（MoS）。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17081
作者: Yixing Li,Ruobing Xie,Xingwu Sun,Yu Cheng,Zhanhui Kang
关键词-EN: garnered significant attention, continuous speech tokenizer, significant attention, speech, continuous speech
类目: ound (cs.SD); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: 4 pages. Under review

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The fusion of speech and language in the era of large language models has garnered significant attention. Discrete speech token is often utilized in text-to-speech tasks for speech compression and portability, which is convenient for joint training with text and have good compression efficiency. However, we found that the discrete speech tokenizer still suffers from information loss. Therefore, we propose a simple yet effective continuous speech tokenizer and a text-to-speech model based on continuous speech tokens. Our results show that the speech language model based on the continuous speech tokenizer has better continuity and higher estimated Mean Opinion Scores (MoS). This enhancement is attributed to better information preservation rate of the continuous speech tokenizer across both low and high frequencies in the frequency domain.
摘要：在大语言模型时代，语音与语言的融合引起了广泛关注。离散语音 Token 常用于文本到语音任务中，以实现语音压缩和便携性，便于与文本联合训练并具有良好的压缩效率。然而，我们发现离散语音 Tokenizer 仍存在信息损失问题。因此，我们提出了一种简单而有效的连续语音 Tokenizer 及基于连续语音 Token 的文本到语音模型。实验结果表明，基于连续语音 Tokenizer 的语音语言模型在连续性和估计的平均意见得分 (MoS) 方面表现更佳。这一改进归因于连续语音 Tokenizer 在频域中低频和高频段的信息保留率更高。

[NLP-28] Data-driven Coreference-based Ontology Building

【速读】： 该论文试图解决从大规模语料库中自动构建领域本体的问题。解决方案的关键在于利用共指消解技术从3000万篇生物医学摘要中提取共指链，并基于这些链构建一个图结构。通过计算节点间的介数中心性，论文能够区分表示层级、同一性和噪声的边，并为层级边分配方向，同时将对应多个不同概念的节点进行拆分。最终，该方法生成了一个数据驱动的生物医学领域本体，部分内容与人工编写的本体高度重叠。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17051
作者: Shir Ashury-Tahan,Amir David Nissan Cohen,Nadav Cohen,Yoram Louzoun,Yoav Goldberg
关键词-EN: individual document understanding, document-level coreference relations, document understanding, resolution is traditionally, component in individual
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:While coreference resolution is traditionally used as a component in individual document understanding, in this work we take a more global view and explore what can we learn about a domain from the set of all document-level coreference relations that are present in a large corpus. We derive coreference chains from a corpus of 30 million biomedical abstracts and construct a graph based on the string phrases within these chains, establishing connections between phrases if they co-occur within the same coreference chain. We then use the graph structure and the betweeness centrality measure to distinguish between edges denoting hierarchy, identity and noise, assign directionality to edges denoting hierarchy, and split nodes (strings) that correspond to multiple distinct concepts. The result is a rich, data-driven ontology over concepts in the biomedical domain, parts of which overlaps significantly with human-authored ontologies. We release the coreference chains and resulting ontology under a creative-commons license, along with the code.
摘要：尽管指代消解传统上被用作单个文档理解的一个组成部分，但在这项工作中，我们采取了一种更全局化的视角，探讨了从大型语料库中所有文档级别的指代关系集合中，我们能学到关于一个领域的哪些知识。我们从包含3000万篇生物医学摘要的语料库中提取指代链，并基于这些链中的字符串短语构建图结构，如果这些短语在同一指代链中共现，则在这些短语之间建立连接。随后，我们利用图结构和介数中心性度量来区分表示层级、同一性和噪声的边，为表示层级的边赋予方向性，并拆分对应于多个不同概念的节点（字符串）。结果是一个丰富的、数据驱动的生物医学领域概念本体，其中部分内容与人工编写的本体显著重叠。我们以创作共用许可证发布指代链和生成的本体，以及相关代码。

[NLP-29] UnStar: Unlearning with Self-Taught Anti-Sample Reasoning for LLMs

【速读】： 该论文试图解决在大语言模型（LLMs）中实现高效、细粒度的有针对性遗忘（unlearning）问题。解决方案的关键在于引入了一种新颖的概念——反样本诱导遗忘（anti-sample-induced unlearning），通过生成误导性理由（misleading rationales）来创建反样本（anti-samples），从而逆转已学习的关联并加速遗忘过程。这种方法不仅实现了细粒度的有针对性遗忘，还能在不影响相关知识的情况下选择性地移除特定关联，这是以往方法无法实现的。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17050
作者: Yash Sinha,Murari Mandal,Mohan Kankanhalli
关键词-EN: reversed loss function, optimizing accuracy, loss function, key components, reversed loss
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The key components of machine learning are data samples for training, model for learning patterns, and loss function for optimizing accuracy. Analogously, unlearning can potentially be achieved through anti-data samples (or anti-samples), unlearning method, and reversed loss function. While prior research has explored unlearning methods and reversed loss functions, the potential of anti-samples remains largely untapped. In this paper, we introduce UnSTAR: Unlearning with Self-Taught Anti-Sample Reasoning for large language models (LLMs). Our contributions are threefold; first, we propose a novel concept of anti-sample-induced unlearning; second, we generate anti-samples by leveraging misleading rationales, which help reverse learned associations and accelerate the unlearning process; and third, we enable fine-grained targeted unlearning, allowing for the selective removal of specific associations without impacting related knowledge - something not achievable by previous works. Results demonstrate that anti-samples offer an efficient, targeted unlearning strategy for LLMs, opening new avenues for privacy-preserving machine learning and model modification.
摘要：机器学习的关键组成部分包括用于训练的数据样本、用于学习模式的学习模型，以及用于优化准确性的损失函数。类似地，遗忘可能通过反数据样本（或反样本）、遗忘方法和反向损失函数来实现。尽管先前的研究已经探索了遗忘方法和反向损失函数，但反样本的潜力在很大程度上仍未被开发。本文中，我们介绍了 UnSTAR：基于自教反样本推理的大语言模型（LLMs）遗忘方法。我们的贡献有三方面：首先，我们提出了反样本诱导遗忘的新概念；其次，我们通过利用误导性理由生成反样本，这有助于逆转学习到的关联并加速遗忘过程；第三，我们实现了细粒度的目标遗忘，允许选择性地移除特定关联而不影响相关知识——这是先前工作无法实现的。结果表明，反样本为 LLMs 提供了一种高效、有针对性的遗忘策略，为隐私保护的机器学习和模型修改开辟了新的途径。

[NLP-30] Arabic Dataset for LLM Safeguard Evaluation

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决大型语言模型（LLMs）在阿拉伯语环境中的安全性问题，特别是考虑到阿拉伯语的复杂语言和文化背景。解决方案的关键在于构建了一个针对阿拉伯地区的特定安全评估数据集，包含5,799个问题，涵盖直接攻击、间接攻击和无害请求，并结合阿拉伯社会的文化背景进行调整。此外，论文提出了一个双视角评估框架，从政府和反对派两个立场评估LLM对敏感和争议话题的处理方式。通过实验，论文揭示了现有阿拉伯语和多语言LLM在安全性表现上的显著差异，强调了开发文化特定数据集以确保LLMs负责任部署的重要性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17040
作者: Yasser Ashraf,Yuxia Wang,Bin Gu,Preslav Nakov,Timothy Baldwin
关键词-EN: large language models, language models, large language, raised concerns, focused on English
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 17 pages, 6 figures, 10 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The growing use of large language models (LLMs) has raised concerns regarding their safety. While many studies have focused on English, the safety of LLMs in Arabic, with its linguistic and cultural complexities, remains under-explored. Here, we aim to bridge this gap. In particular, we present an Arab-region-specific safety evaluation dataset consisting of 5,799 questions, including direct attacks, indirect attacks, and harmless requests with sensitive words, adapted to reflect the socio-cultural context of the Arab world. To uncover the impact of different stances in handling sensitive and controversial topics, we propose a dual-perspective evaluation framework. It assesses the LLM responses from both governmental and opposition viewpoints. Experiments over five leading Arabic-centric and multilingual LLMs reveal substantial disparities in their safety performance. This reinforces the need for culturally specific datasets to ensure the responsible deployment of LLMs.
摘要：随着大语言模型 (LLM) 的广泛应用，其安全性问题引起了广泛关注。尽管许多研究聚焦于英语，但阿拉伯语大语言模型的安全性，因其语言和文化的复杂性，仍未得到充分探索。本文旨在填补这一空白。我们特别提出了一个针对阿拉伯地区的安全性评估数据集，包含 5,799 个问题，涵盖直接攻击、间接攻击以及带有敏感词汇的无害请求，这些问题均根据阿拉伯世界的社会文化背景进行了调整。为了揭示处理敏感和争议话题时不同立场的影响，我们提出了一种双重视角评估框架，从政府和反对派两个角度评估 LLM 的响应。通过对五个领先的以阿拉伯语为中心和多语言 LLM 的实验，我们发现它们在安全性表现上存在显著差异。这进一步强调了需要特定文化背景的数据集，以确保 LLM 的负责任部署。

[NLP-31] DIRI: Adversarial Patient Reidentification with Large Language Models for Evaluating Clinical Text Anonymization

【速读】： 该论文试图解决当前去识别化（deidentification）方法在实际临床文本中的有效性问题，特别是在高度饱和的数据集上表现良好，但在真实世界复杂文本中可能存在显著弱点。解决方案的关键在于开发了一种对抗性方法，利用大型语言模型（LLM）对经过去识别化处理的临床笔记进行再识别（re-identification），并通过De-Identification/Re-Identification（DIRI）方法评估其性能。该方法揭示了现有去识别化技术的不足，并为迭代改进提供了工具。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17035
作者: John X. Morris,Thomas R. Campion,Sri Laasya Nutheti,Yifan Peng,Akhil Raj,Ramin Zabih,Curtis L. Cole
关键词-EN: Sharing protected health, protected health information, furthering biomedical research, Sharing protected, health information
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Sharing protected health information (PHI) is critical for furthering biomedical research. Before data can be distributed, practitioners often perform deidentification to remove any PHI contained in the text. Contemporary deidentification methods are evaluated on highly saturated datasets (tools achieve near-perfect accuracy) which may not reflect the full variability or complexity of real-world clinical text and annotating them is resource intensive, which is a barrier to real-world applications. To address this gap, we developed an adversarial approach using a large language model (LLM) to re-identify the patient corresponding to a redacted clinical note and evaluated the performance with a novel De-Identification/Re-Identification (DIRI) method. Our method uses a large language model to reidentify the patient corresponding to a redacted clinical note. We demonstrate our method on medical data from Weill Cornell Medicine anonymized with three deidentification tools: rule-based Philter and two deep-learning-based models, BiLSTM-CRF and ClinicalBERT. Although ClinicalBERT was the most effective, masking all identified PII, our tool still reidentified 9% of clinical notes Our study highlights significant weaknesses in current deidentification technologies while providing a tool for iterative development and improvement.
摘要：共享受保护的健康信息 (PHI) 对于推进生物医学研究至关重要。在数据分发之前，从业者通常会进行去识别化处理，以移除文本中包含的任何 PHI。当代的去识别化方法在高度饱和的数据集上进行评估（工具达到近乎完美的准确率），这可能无法反映现实世界临床文本的全部变异性或复杂性，并且对这些数据进行注释需要大量资源，这是实际应用中的一个障碍。为了解决这一差距，我们开发了一种使用大语言模型 (LLM) 的对抗性方法，以重新识别与经过编辑的临床笔记相对应的患者，并使用一种新颖的去识别/重新识别 (DIRI) 方法评估其性能。我们的方法使用大语言模型重新识别与经过编辑的临床笔记相对应的患者。我们在来自 Weill Cornell Medicine 的医疗数据上展示了我们的方法，这些数据通过三种去识别化工具进行了匿名化处理：基于规则的 Philter 和两种基于深度学习的模型，BiLSTM-CRF 和 ClinicalBERT。尽管 ClinicalBERT 最为有效，掩盖了所有识别到的 PII，但我们的工具仍然重新识别了 9% 的临床笔记。我们的研究突显了当前去识别化技术的显著弱点，同时提供了一个用于迭代开发和改进的工具。

[NLP-32] SG-FSM: A Self-Guiding Zero-Shot Prompting Paradigm for Multi-Hop Question Answering Based on Finite State Machine

【速读】： 该论文试图解决多跳问答（Multi-hop Question Answering, MHQA）任务中存在的幻觉、错误传播和上下文长度限制等问题。解决方案的关键在于提出了自引导提示有限状态机（Self-Guiding prompting Finite State Machine, SG-FSM），通过将复杂问题迭代分解为子问题，并根据当前上下文和结果动态决定下一步操作，从而增强多跳推理能力。SG-FSM能够减少幻觉现象，即使在中间步骤出错的情况下也能恢复正确的最终答案，并提高输出格式的准确性，简化评估过程。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17021
作者: Xiaochen Wang,Junqing He,Liang Chen,Reza Haf Zhe Yang,Yiru Wang,Xiangdi Meng,Kunhao Pan,Zhifang Sui
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, language inference tasks, natural language inference, Large Language, shown impressive capabilities
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models with chain-of-thought prompting, such as OpenAI-o1, have shown impressive capabilities in natural language inference tasks. However, Multi-hop Question Answering (MHQA) remains challenging for many existing models due to issues like hallucination, error propagation, and limited context length. To address these challenges and enhance LLMs’ performance on MHQA, we propose the Self-Guiding prompting Finite State Machine (SG-FSM), designed to strengthen multi-hop reasoning abilities. Unlike traditional chain-of-thought methods, SG-FSM tackles MHQA by iteratively breaking down complex questions into sub-questions, correcting itself to improve accuracy. It processes one sub-question at a time, dynamically deciding the next step based on the current context and results, functioning much like an automaton. Experiments across various benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, outperforming strong baselines on challenging datasets such as Musique. SG-FSM reduces hallucination, enabling recovery of the correct final answer despite intermediate errors. It also improves adherence to specified output formats, simplifying evaluation significantly.
摘要：具有链式思维提示的大语言模型，如 OpenAI-o1，在自然语言推理任务中展现了令人印象深刻的能力。然而，多跳问答 (Multi-hop Question Answering, MHQA) 对许多现有模型来说仍然是一个挑战，主要问题包括幻觉 (hallucination)、错误传播 (error propagation) 和有限的上下文长度 (limited context length)。为了应对这些挑战并提升大语言模型在 MHQA 上的表现，我们提出了自引导提示有限状态机 (Self-Guiding prompting Finite State Machine, SG-FSM)，旨在增强多跳推理能力。与传统的链式思维方法不同，SG-FSM 通过迭代地将复杂问题分解为子问题，并自我修正以提高准确性来解决 MHQA 问题。它一次处理一个子问题，根据当前上下文和结果动态决定下一步，其运作方式类似于自动机。在各种基准测试中的实验结果表明，我们的方法具有显著的有效性，在诸如 Musique 这样的挑战性数据集上超越了强大的基线模型。SG-FSM 减少了幻觉现象，即使在中间步骤出现错误的情况下，也能恢复正确的最终答案。它还提高了对指定输出格式的遵循程度，显著简化了评估过程。

[NLP-33] Exploring Forgetting in Large Language Model Pre-Training

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大语言模型（LLMs）在预训练阶段存在的灾难性遗忘问题。解决方案的关键在于系统性地探索和测量预训练中的遗忘现象，质疑传统的困惑度（PPL）等指标，并引入新的度量方法以更好地检测实体记忆的保留情况。论文提出了一系列低成本、直接的方法来缓解预训练阶段的遗忘，并通过分析学习曲线揭示了遗忘的动态特性，为未来LLMs的研究提供了重要参考。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17018
作者: Chonghua Liao,Ruobing Xie,Xingwu Sun,Haowen Sun,Zhanhui Kang
关键词-EN: Catastrophic forgetting remains, large language models, Catastrophic forgetting, omniscient model, language models
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Catastrophic forgetting remains a formidable obstacle to building an omniscient model in large language models (LLMs). Despite the pioneering research on task-level forgetting in LLM fine-tuning, there is scant focus on forgetting during pre-training. We systematically explored the existence and measurement of forgetting in pre-training, questioning traditional metrics such as perplexity (PPL) and introducing new metrics to better detect entity memory retention. Based on our revised assessment of forgetting metrics, we explored low-cost, straightforward methods to mitigate forgetting during the pre-training phase. Further, we carefully analyzed the learning curves, offering insights into the dynamics of forgetting. Extensive evaluations and analyses on forgetting of pre-training could facilitate future research on LLMs.
摘要：灾难性遗忘仍然是构建全知大语言模型 (LLM) 的一大障碍。尽管在 LLM 微调中对任务级遗忘的研究已取得开创性进展，但在预训练阶段的遗忘问题却鲜有关注。我们系统地探讨了预训练中遗忘的存在及其测量方法，质疑了诸如困惑度 (PPL) 等传统指标，并引入了新的指标以更好地检测实体记忆的保留情况。基于我们对遗忘指标的修正评估，我们探索了在预训练阶段缓解遗忘的低成本、直接方法。此外，我们仔细分析了学习曲线，提供了关于遗忘动态的见解。对预训练遗忘的广泛评估和分析将有助于未来对 LLM 的研究。

[NLP-34] IPL: Leveraging Multimodal Large Language Models for Intelligent Product Listing

【速读】： 该论文试图解决C2C平台中个体卖家在产品描述撰写方面的困难，特别是由于缺乏电商经验导致的产品描述质量不高的问题。解决方案的关键在于开发了IPL（Intelligent Product Listing）工具，该工具利用多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）生成基于产品属性的描述，并通过领域特定的指令微调和多模态检索增强生成（RAG）技术，模仿C2C平台Xianyu的内容风格。IPL通过用户上传的产品照片自动生成描述，显著提升了产品列表的质量和用户满意度。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16977
作者: Kang Chen,Qingheng Zhang,Chengbao Lian,Yixin Ji,Xuwei Liu,Shuguang Han,Guoqiang Wu,Fei Huang,Jufeng Chen
关键词-EN: lack sufficient experience, Unlike professional, Facebook marketplace, targeting individual sellers, Multimodal Large Language
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Unlike professional Business-to-Consumer (B2C) e-commerce platforms (e.g., Amazon), Consumer-to-Consumer (C2C) platforms (e.g., Facebook marketplace) are mainly targeting individual sellers who usually lack sufficient experience in e-commerce. Individual sellers often struggle to compose proper descriptions for selling products. With the recent advancement of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), we attempt to integrate such state-of-the-art generative AI technologies into the product listing process. To this end, we develop IPL, an Intelligent Product Listing tool tailored to generate descriptions using various product attributes such as category, brand, color, condition, etc. IPL enables users to compose product descriptions by merely uploading photos of the selling product. More importantly, it can imitate the content style of our C2C platform Xianyu. This is achieved by employing domain-specific instruction tuning on MLLMs and adopting the multi-modal Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) process. A comprehensive empirical evaluation demonstrates that the underlying model of IPL significantly outperforms the base model in domain-specific tasks while producing less hallucination. IPL has been successfully deployed in our production system, where 72% of users have their published product listings based on the generated content, and those product listings are shown to have a quality score 5.6% higher than those without AI assistance.
摘要：与专业的企业对消费者 (B2C) 电子商务平台（例如 Amazon）不同，消费者对消费者 (C2C) 平台（例如 Facebook 市场）主要面向通常缺乏电子商务经验的个人卖家。个人卖家往往难以撰写合适的产品描述。随着多模态大语言模型 (MLLMs) 的最新进展，我们尝试将这些先进的生成式 AI 技术整合到产品上架流程中。为此，我们开发了 IPL，一种智能产品上架工具，专门用于根据类别、品牌、颜色、状态等各种产品属性生成描述。IPL 使用户只需上传待售产品的照片即可撰写产品描述。更重要的是，它能够模仿我们 C2C 平台闲鱼的文案风格。这是通过在 MLLMs 上进行领域特定的指令调优，并采用多模态检索增强生成 (RAG) 流程实现的。全面的实证评估表明，IPL 的基础模型在领域特定任务中显著优于基础模型，同时产生的幻觉更少。IPL 已成功部署在我们的生产系统中，其中 72% 的用户发布的产品上架内容基于生成的内容，并且这些产品上架的质量评分比没有 AI 辅助的高出 5.6%。

[NLP-35] Learning Mathematical Rules with Large Language Models NEURIPS’24

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大型语言模型在学习和应用特定数学规则（如分配律或方程简化）方面的能力问题。解决方案的关键在于通过严格的合成数据构建方法，将这些数学规则嵌入数据中，并对大型语言模型进行微调。实验结果表明，该模型能够在一定程度上学习并泛化这些规则，并在解决实际问题时适当地重用这些规则。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16973
作者: Antoine Gorceix,Bastien Le Chenadec,Ahmad Rammal,Nelson Vadori,Manuela Veloso
关键词-EN: specific mathematical rules, learn specific mathematical, large language models, simplifying equations, specific mathematical
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 4th MATH-AI Workshop at NeurIPS’24

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In this paper, we study the ability of large language models to learn specific mathematical rules such as distributivity or simplifying equations. We present an empirical analysis of their ability to generalize these rules, as well as to reuse them in the context of word problems. For this purpose, we provide a rigorous methodology to build synthetic data incorporating such rules, and perform fine-tuning of large language models on such data. Our experiments show that our model can learn and generalize these rules to some extent, as well as suitably reuse them in the context of word problems.
摘要：本文研究了大语言模型学习特定数学规则（如分配律或简化方程）的能力。我们对其泛化这些规则的能力以及在文字问题情境中重用这些规则的能力进行了实证分析。为此，我们提出了一种严格的合成数据构建方法，将这些规则融入其中，并对大语言模型进行了微调。实验结果表明，我们的模型能够在一定程度上学习并泛化这些规则，并在文字问题情境中适当地重用它们。
