LBLM-AVA incorporates several key components enhancing its gesture generation capabilities, such as multimodal-to-pose embeddings, enhanced sequence-to-sequence mapping with redefined attention mechanisms, a temporal smoothing module for gesture sequence coherence, and an attention-based refinement module for enhanced realism. The model is trained on our large-scale proprietary open-source dataset Allo-AVA. LBLM-AVA achieves state-of-the-art performance in generating lifelike and contextually appropriate gestures with a 30% reduction in Fréchet Gesture Distance (FGD), and a 25% improvement in Fréchet Inception Distance compared to existing approaches.
摘要：随着虚拟智能体在人机交互中的日益普及，实时生成逼真且上下文相关的动作仍然是一个重大挑战。尽管神经渲染技术在静态脚本处理方面取得了显著进展，但其在人机交互中的应用仍然有限。为此，我们引入了大型身体语言模型 (Large Body Language Models, LBLMs)，并提出了 LBLM-AVA，这是一种新颖的 LBLM 架构，结合了 Transformer-XL 大语言模型与并行扩散模型，以从多模态输入 (文本、音频和视频) 生成类人动作。LBLM-AVA 集成了多个关键组件以增强其动作生成能力，例如多模态到姿态嵌入、通过重新定义注意力机制增强的序列到序列映射、用于动作序列连贯性的时间平滑模块，以及基于注意力的细化模块以增强真实感。该模型在我们的全规模专有开源数据集 Allo-AVA 上进行了训练。LBLM-AVA 在生成逼真且上下文相关的动作方面达到了最先进的性能，相较于现有方法，Fréchet 动作距离 (FGD) 减少了 30%，Fréchet Inception 距离提高了 25%。

[NLP-70] Bayesian scaling laws for in-context learning

【速读】： 该论文试图解释上下文学习（In-context Learning, ICL）中示例数量与模型预测准确性之间的相关性，并提出一种新的解释框架，即ICL近似于贝叶斯学习者。解决方案的关键在于提出了基于贝叶斯视角的ICL缩放定律，这些定律不仅在准确性上超越或匹配现有定律，还提供了对任务先验、学习效率和每个示例概率的可解释性。通过实验验证，这些贝叶斯缩放定律能够准确预测ICL在特定条件下导致被抑制行为重新出现的情况，从而揭示了训练后增加大语言模型（LLM）安全性的无效性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16531
作者: Aryaman Arora,Dan Jurafsky,Christopher Potts,Noah D. Goodman
关键词-EN: perform complex tasks, Bayesian scaling laws, training updates, scaling laws, powerful technique
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Formal Languages and Automata Theory (cs.FL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 10 pages main text, 26 pages total

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In-context learning (ICL) is a powerful technique for getting language models to perform complex tasks with no training updates. Prior work has established strong correlations between the number of in-context examples provided and the accuracy of the model’s predictions. In this paper, we seek to explain this correlation by showing that ICL approximates a Bayesian learner. This perspective gives rise to a family of novel Bayesian scaling laws for ICL. In experiments with \mboxGPT-2 models of different sizes, our scaling laws exceed or match existing scaling laws in accuracy while also offering interpretable terms for task priors, learning efficiency, and per-example probabilities. To illustrate the analytic power that such interpretable scaling laws provide, we report on controlled synthetic dataset experiments designed to inform real-world studies of safety alignment. In our experimental protocol, we use SFT to suppress an unwanted existing model capability and then use ICL to try to bring that capability back (many-shot jailbreaking). We then experiment on real-world instruction-tuned LLMs using capabilities benchmarks as well as a new many-shot jailbreaking dataset. In all cases, Bayesian scaling laws accurately predict the conditions under which ICL will cause the suppressed behavior to reemerge, which sheds light on the ineffectiveness of post-training at increasing LLM safety.
摘要：上下文学习 (In-context Learning, ICL) 是一种强大的技术，能够让语言模型在无需训练更新的情况下执行复杂任务。先前的工作已经确立了提供的上下文示例数量与模型预测准确性之间的强相关性。本文旨在通过展示 ICL 近似于贝叶斯学习者来解释这种相关性。这一视角引出了一组新颖的贝叶斯缩放定律 (Bayesian Scaling Laws) 用于 ICL。在不同大小的 GPT-2 模型实验中，我们的缩放定律在准确性上超越或匹配现有的缩放定律，同时提供了可解释的任务先验、学习效率和每个示例概率的术语。为了展示这种可解释缩放定律的分析能力，我们报告了旨在为现实世界的安全对齐研究提供信息的受控合成数据集实验。在我们的实验协议中，我们使用 SFT 抑制现有模型的不良能力，然后使用 ICL 尝试恢复该能力（多样本越狱）。随后，我们在现实世界的指令微调大语言模型 (Large Language Models, LLMs) 上进行实验，使用能力基准以及一个新的多样本越狱数据集。在所有情况下，贝叶斯缩放定律都能准确预测 ICL 导致被抑制行为重新出现的条件，这揭示了训练后增加 LLM 安全性的无效性。

[NLP-71] AUTALIC: A Dataset for Anti-AUTistic Ableist Language In Context

【速读】： 该论文试图解决在自然语言处理（NLP）研究中检测反自闭症歧视性语言的挑战，这一问题由于其微妙且依赖上下文的特性而未得到充分探索。解决方案的关键在于提出了AUTALIC，这是首个专门用于检测反自闭症歧视性语言的基准数据集。该数据集包含从Reddit收集的2,400个与自闭症相关的句子及其上下文，并由具有神经多样性背景的专家进行标注。通过全面评估，论文揭示了当前语言模型，包括最先进的LLMs，在识别反自闭症歧视性语言方面存在显著困难，无法与人类判断完全一致。AUTALIC的发布为研究人员提供了宝贵的资源，有助于推动开发更具包容性和上下文感知能力的NLP系统，从而更好地反映多样化的视角。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16520
作者: Naba Rizvi,Harper Strickland,Daniel Gitelman,Tristan Cooper,Alexis Morales-Flores,Michael Golden,Aekta Kallepalli,Akshat Alurkar,Haaset Owens,Saleha Ahmedi,Isha Khirwadkar,Imani Munyaka,Nedjma Ousidhoum
关键词-EN: understanding of autism, continues to increase, autistic people, ableist language, understanding
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: 9 pages, 5 figures, 7 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:As our understanding of autism and ableism continues to increase, so does our understanding of ableist language towards autistic people. Such language poses a significant challenge in NLP research due to its subtle and context-dependent nature. Yet, detecting anti-autistic ableist language remains underexplored, with existing NLP tools often failing to capture its nuanced expressions. We present AUTALIC, the first benchmark dataset dedicated to the detection of anti-autistic ableist language in context, addressing a significant gap in the field. The dataset comprises 2,400 autism-related sentences collected from Reddit, accompanied by surrounding context, and is annotated by trained experts with backgrounds in neurodiversity. Our comprehensive evaluation reveals that current language models, including state-of-the-art LLMs, struggle to reliably identify anti-autistic ableism and align with human judgments, underscoring their limitations in this domain. We publicly release AUTALIC along with the individual annotations which serve as a valuable resource to researchers working on ableism, neurodiversity, and also studying disagreements in annotation tasks. This dataset serves as a crucial step towards developing more inclusive and context-aware NLP systems that better reflect diverse perspectives.
摘要：随着我们对自闭症和残疾歧视的理解不断加深，我们对针对自闭症患者的残疾歧视语言的理解也在增加。这种语言由于其微妙且依赖上下文的特点，给自然语言处理 (NLP) 研究带来了重大挑战。然而，检测反自闭症的残疾歧视语言仍未得到充分探索，现有的 NLP 工具往往无法捕捉其细微的表达。我们提出了 AUTALIC，这是首个专门用于检测上下文中反自闭症残疾歧视语言的基准数据集，填补了该领域的重大空白。该数据集包含从 Reddit 收集的 2,400 个与自闭症相关的句子，并附有周围上下文，由具有神经多样性背景的训练有素的专家进行标注。我们的全面评估显示，包括最先进的大语言模型 (LLM) 在内的当前语言模型，在可靠识别反自闭症残疾歧视语言和与人类判断保持一致方面存在困难，突显了它们在这一领域的局限性。我们公开发布了 AUTALIC 及其个体标注，这些标注为从事残疾歧视、神经多样性研究以及研究标注任务中分歧的研究人员提供了宝贵的资源。该数据集是开发更具包容性和上下文感知的 NLP 系统的重要一步，这些系统能更好地反映多样化的视角。

[NLP-72] Learning from others mistakes: Finetuning machine translation models with span-level error annotations

【速读】： 该论文试图解决如何利用细粒度的跨度级标注（span-level annotations）来提升语言模型质量的问题。解决方案的关键在于开发了一种名为“带标注训练”（Training with Annotations, TWA）的微调算法，该算法能够直接在带有跨度级错误信息的标注数据上训练机器翻译模型。TWA不仅利用了目标跨度内的错误信息，还能灵活地学习在跨度内应惩罚的内容，同时考虑序列的整体轨迹来决定哪些非错误跨度可以作为正信号使用。实验结果表明，TWA在英德和汉英机器翻译任务中优于传统的监督微调和直接偏好优化等基线方法。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16509
作者: Lily H. Zhang,Hamid Dadkhahi,Mara Finkelstein,Firas Trabelsi,Jiaming Luo,Markus Freitag
关键词-EN: improve language models, growing interest, interest in incorporating, incorporating feedback, efforts focus
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Despite growing interest in incorporating feedback to improve language models, most efforts focus only on sequence-level annotations. In this work, we explore the potential of utilizing fine-grained span-level annotations from offline datasets to improve model quality. We develop a simple finetuning algorithm, called Training with Annotations (TWA), to directly train machine translation models on such annotated data. TWA utilizes targeted span-level error information while also flexibly learning what to penalize within a span. Moreover, TWA considers the overall trajectory of a sequence when deciding which non-error spans to utilize as positive signals. Experiments on English-German and Chinese-English machine translation show that TWA outperforms baselines such as Supervised FineTuning on sequences filtered for quality and Direct Preference Optimization on pairs constructed from the same data.
摘要：尽管越来越多的人关注通过反馈来改进语言模型，但大多数努力仅集中在序列级别的标注上。在本研究中，我们探讨了利用离线数据集中细粒度的跨级别标注来提升模型质量的潜力。我们开发了一种名为“带标注训练 (Training with Annotations, TWA)”的简单微调算法，直接在经过标注的数据上训练机器翻译模型。TWA 利用了目标跨级别的错误信息，同时灵活地学习在跨内惩罚什么。此外，TWA 在决定使用哪些非错误跨作为正信号时，考虑了序列的整体轨迹。在英德和汉英机器翻译实验中，TWA 的表现优于基于质量筛选序列的监督微调 (Supervised FineTuning) 和基于相同数据构建对的直接偏好优化 (Direct Preference Optimization) 等基线方法。

[NLP-73] Allo-AVA: A Large-Scale Multimodal Conversational AI Dataset for Allocentric Avatar Gesture Animation

【速读】： 该论文试图解决虚拟环境中对话式AI角色动画生成中高质量多模态训练数据的稀缺问题。解决方案的关键在于引入Allo-AVA数据集，该数据集包含约1,250小时的多样化视频内容，结合音频、转录文本和提取的关键点，并精确映射这些关键点到时间戳，从而实现语音与身体及面部动作的同步复制。这一全面资源为开发和评估更自然、上下文感知的角色动画模型提供了基础，有望在虚拟现实和数字助手等应用中实现革命性变革。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16503
作者: Saif Punjwani,Larry Heck
关键词-EN: multimodal training data, training data severely, data severely hinders, scarcity of high-quality, multimodal training
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The scarcity of high-quality, multimodal training data severely hinders the creation of lifelike avatar animations for conversational AI in virtual environments. Existing datasets often lack the intricate synchronization between speech, facial expressions, and body movements that characterize natural human communication. To address this critical gap, we introduce Allo-AVA, a large-scale dataset specifically designed for text and audio-driven avatar gesture animation in an allocentric (third person point-of-view) context. Allo-AVA consists of \sim 1,250 hours of diverse video content, complete with audio, transcripts, and extracted keypoints. Allo-AVA uniquely maps these keypoints to precise timestamps, enabling accurate replication of human movements (body and facial gestures) in synchronization with speech. This comprehensive resource enables the development and evaluation of more natural, context-aware avatar animation models, potentially transforming applications ranging from virtual reality to digital assistants.
摘要：高质量多模态训练数据的稀缺性严重阻碍了虚拟环境中对话式 AI 虚拟形象动画的创建。现有数据集往往缺乏语音、面部表情和身体动作之间精细同步的特点，这些特点是自然人类交流的特征。为了解决这一关键差距，我们引入了 Allo-AVA，这是一个专为以自我为中心（第三人称视角）情境下的文本和音频驱动的虚拟形象手势动画设计的大规模数据集。Allo-AVA 包含约 1,250 小时的多样化视频内容，配有音频、转录文本和提取的关键点。Allo-AVA 独特地将这些关键点映射到精确的时间戳，使得能够准确复制与语音同步的人类动作（身体和面部手势）。这一全面资源促进了更自然、更具上下文感知能力的虚拟形象动画模型的开发和评估，有望在虚拟现实到数字助手等多个应用领域带来变革。

[NLP-74] Rulebreakers Challenge: Revealing a Blind Spot in Large Language Models Reasoning with Formal Logic

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大型语言模型（LLMs）在区分逻辑推理中的“规则破坏者”（rulebreakers）与非规则破坏者时表现不佳的问题。解决方案的关键在于引入了一个名为RULEBREAKERS的新数据集，用于评估LLMs在这方面的能力。通过聚焦于modus tollens和disjunctive syllogism这两种逻辑推理形式，研究评估了六种最先进的LLMs，并发现尽管大多数模型在识别规则破坏者方面表现不佳，但它们在通过模型置信度评估时显示出潜在的区分能力。进一步分析表明，模型未能识别规则破坏者可能与其世界知识和注意力分布模式有关。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16502
作者: Jason Chan,Robert Gaizauskas,Zhixue Zhao
关键词-EN: Formal logic, logically entailed, inferred by humans, natural language reasoning, logic has long
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Preprint

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Formal logic has long been applied to natural language reasoning, but this approach can sometimes lead to conclusions that, while logically entailed, are factually inconsistent with the premises or are not typically inferred by humans. This study introduces the concept of “rulebreakers”, which refers to instances where logical entailment diverges from factually acceptable inference. We present RULEBREAKERS, a novel dataset for evaluating Large Language Models’ (LLMs) ability to distinguish between rulebreakers and non-rulebreakers. Focusing on modus tollens and disjunctive syllogism, we assess six state-of-the-art LLMs using RULEBREAKERS, measuring their performance in terms of token-level exact accuracy and model confidence. Our findings reveal that while most models perform poorly to moderately in recognizing rulebreakers, they demonstrate a latent ability to distinguish rulebreakers when assessed by their confidence levels. Further analysis suggests that the failure to recognize rulebreakers is potentially associated with the models’ world knowledge and their attention distribution patterns. This research highlights the limitation of LLMs’ reasoning capabilities, and contributes to the ongoing discussion on reasoning in LLMs.
摘要：形式逻辑长期以来被应用于自然语言推理，但这种方法有时会导致虽然逻辑上蕴含但与前提事实不一致或非人类通常推断的结论。本研究引入了“规则破坏者”的概念，指的是逻辑蕴含与事实可接受的推理相背离的情况。我们提出了 RULEBREAKERS，这是一个用于评估大语言模型 (LLMs) 区分规则破坏者和非规则破坏者能力的新数据集。聚焦于否定后件推理和析取三段论，我们使用 RULEBREAKERS 评估了六个最先进的 LLMs，测量它们在 Token 级别上的精确准确性和模型置信度。我们的研究结果显示，尽管大多数模型在识别规则破坏者方面表现不佳至中等，但当通过其置信度水平评估时，它们展现出区分规则破坏者的潜在能力。进一步分析表明，未能识别规则破坏者可能与模型的世界知识和其注意力分布模式有关。这项研究突显了 LLMs 推理能力的局限性，并为 LLMs 推理的持续讨论做出了贡献。

[NLP-75] Natural Language Processing for Human Resources: A Survey

【速读】： 该论文旨在探讨自然语言处理（NLP）技术在人力资源（HR）领域的应用，并解决该领域中资源获取困难和问题复杂性等挑战。解决方案的关键在于识别和阐述HR领域中的关键NLP任务，如技能提取，以及这些任务如何服务于更广泛的HR目标，如职位匹配。通过提供一个从NLP视角出发的HR任务概览，论文为NLP研究人员和从业者指明了未来研究的方向和潜在机会。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16498
作者: Naoki Otani,Nikita Bhutani,Estevam Hruschka
关键词-EN: natural language processing, includes a broad, language processing, broad spectrum, related to natural
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The domain of human resources (HR) includes a broad spectrum of tasks related to natural language processing (NLP) techniques. Recent breakthroughs in NLP have generated significant interest in its industrial applications in this domain and potentially alleviate challenges such as the difficulty of resource acquisition and the complexity of problems. At the same time, the HR domain can also present unique challenges that drive state-of-the-art in NLP research. To support this, we provide NLP researchers and practitioners with an overview of key HR tasks from an NLP perspective, illustrating how specific sub-tasks (e.g., skill extraction) contribute to broader objectives (e.g., job matching). Through this survey, we identify opportunities in NLP for HR and suggest directions for future exploration.
摘要：人力资源 (HR) 领域涵盖了与自然语言处理 (NLP) 技术相关的广泛任务。NLP 领域的最新突破引发了对其在该领域工业应用的极大兴趣，并有望缓解资源获取困难和问题复杂性等挑战。与此同时，HR 领域也带来了独特的挑战，推动了 NLP 研究的最前沿。为此，我们为 NLP 研究人员和从业者提供了从 NLP 角度对关键 HR 任务的概述，展示了特定子任务（如技能提取）如何促进更广泛的目标（如职位匹配）。通过这项调查，我们识别了 NLP 在 HR 领域的机会，并提出了未来探索的方向。

[NLP-76] BIG5-CHAT: Shaping LLM Personalities Through Training on Human-Grounded Data

【速读】： 该论文试图解决将现实人类个性特征嵌入到大型语言模型（LLMs）中的挑战。解决方案的关键在于引入BIG5-CHAT数据集，该数据集包含100,000个对话，旨在基于人类在文本中表达个性的方式来训练模型。通过利用这一数据集，论文探索了监督微调（Supervised Fine-Tuning）和直接偏好优化（Direct Preference Optimization）两种基于训练的方法，以更自然地使LLMs与人类个性模式对齐。这些方法在个性评估（如BFI和IPIP-NEO）中表现优于基于提示的方法，并且与人类数据的特质相关性更紧密。此外，实验表明，在模型中表现出更高尽责性、更高宜人性、更低外向性和更低神经质性的模型在推理任务中表现更好，这与心理学研究中这些特质对人类认知表现的影响相一致。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16491
作者: Wenkai Li,Jiarui Liu,Andy Liu,Xuhui Zhou,Mona Diab,Maarten Sap
关键词-EN: embedding realistic human, tackle the challenge, challenge of embedding, embedding realistic, Direct Preference Optimization
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In this work, we tackle the challenge of embedding realistic human personality traits into LLMs. Previous approaches have primarily focused on prompt-based methods that describe the behavior associated with the desired personality traits, suffering from realism and validity issues. To address these limitations, we introduce BIG5-CHAT, a large-scale dataset containing 100,000 dialogues designed to ground models in how humans express their personality in text. Leveraging this dataset, we explore Supervised Fine-Tuning and Direct Preference Optimization as training-based methods to align LLMs more naturally with human personality patterns. Our methods outperform prompting on personality assessments such as BFI and IPIP-NEO, with trait correlations more closely matching human data. Furthermore, our experiments reveal that models trained to exhibit higher conscientiousness, higher agreeableness, lower extraversion, and lower neuroticism display better performance on reasoning tasks, aligning with psychological findings on how these traits impact human cognitive performance. To our knowledge, this work is the first comprehensive study to demonstrate how training-based methods can shape LLM personalities through learning from real human behaviors.
摘要：在本研究中，我们致力于解决将现实人类个性特征嵌入到大语言模型 (LLM) 中的挑战。以往的方法主要集中在基于提示的方法上，这些方法描述了与所需个性特征相关的行为，但存在现实性和有效性问题。为了克服这些局限，我们引入了 BIG5-CHAT，这是一个包含 100,000 个对话的大型数据集，旨在使模型基于人类如何在文本中表达其个性。利用该数据集，我们探索了监督微调 (Supervised Fine-Tuning) 和直接偏好优化 (Direct Preference Optimization) 作为基于训练的方法，以更自然地使 LLM 与人类个性模式对齐。我们的方法在 BFI 和 IPIP-NEO 等人格评估中优于提示方法，特质相关性更接近人类数据。此外，我们的实验表明，经过训练表现出更高尽责性、更高宜人性、更低外向性和更低神经质性的模型在推理任务中表现更好，这与心理学研究中关于这些特质如何影响人类认知表现的发现相一致。据我们所知，本研究是首次全面展示如何通过从真实人类行为中学习来塑造 LLM 个性的研究。

[NLP-77] Multi-head Sequence Tagging Model for Grammatical Error Correction

【速读】： 该论文试图解决语法错误纠正（GEC）问题，特别是通过简化从序列到序列（Seq2Seq）模型到序列标注模型的转换，以处理大规模编辑空间中的编辑命令。解决方案的关键在于将GEC任务进一步细分为七个相关子任务（插入、删除、合并、替换、转换、检测和纠正），并为每个子任务设计独立的分类头，从而构建一个多任务学习模型。此外，论文提出了一种新的去噪自编码器来生成合成数据集以增强预训练，并引入字符级转换来提升序列到编辑功能的性能和词汇覆盖率。这些创新使得模型在BEA-19和CoNLL-14测试集上取得了显著优于现有最先进方法的性能。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16473
作者: Kamal Al-Sabahi,Kang Yang,Wangwang Liu,Guanyu Jiang,Xian Li,Ming Yang
关键词-EN: Grammatical Error Correction, source sequence, sequence tagging models, sequence tagging, Error Correction
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:To solve the Grammatical Error Correction (GEC) problem , a mapping between a source sequence and a target one is needed, where the two differ only on few spans. For this reason, the attention has been shifted to the non-autoregressive or sequence tagging models. In which, the GEC has been simplified from Seq2Seq to labeling the input tokens with edit commands chosen from a large edit space. Due to this large number of classes and the limitation of the available datasets, the current sequence tagging approaches still have some issues handling a broad range of grammatical errors just by being laser-focused on one single task. To this end, we simplified the GEC further by dividing it into seven related subtasks: Insertion, Deletion, Merge, Substitution, Transformation, Detection, and Correction, with Correction being our primary focus. A distinct classification head is dedicated to each of these subtasks. the novel multi-head and multi-task learning model is proposed to effectively utilize training data and harness the information from related task training signals. To mitigate the limited number of available training samples, a new denoising autoencoder is used to generate a new synthetic dataset to be used for pretraining. Additionally, a new character-level transformation is proposed to enhance the sequence-to-edit function and improve the model’s vocabulary coverage. Our single/ensemble model achieves an F0.5 of 74.4/77.0, and 68.6/69.1 on BEA-19 (test) and CoNLL-14 (test) respectively. Moreover, evaluated on JFLEG test set, the GLEU scores are 61.6 and 61.7 for the single and ensemble models, respectively. It mostly outperforms recently published state-of-the-art results by a considerable margin.
摘要：为了解决语法错误纠正 (Grammatical Error Correction, GEC) 问题，需要建立源序列与目标序列之间的映射关系，其中两者仅在少数片段上存在差异。因此，研究重点已转向非自回归或序列标注模型。在这些模型中，GEC 问题已从序列到序列 (Seq2Seq) 简化为对输入 Token 进行编辑命令标注，这些命令从庞大的编辑空间中选择。由于类别数量庞大且可用数据集的限制，当前的序列标注方法在处理广泛范围的语法错误时仍存在一些问题，仅专注于单一任务。为此，我们将 GEC 进一步简化为七个相关子任务：插入 (Insertion)、删除 (Deletion)、合并 (Merge)、替换 (Substitution)、转换 (Transformation)、检测 (Detection) 和纠正 (Correction)，其中纠正是我们主要关注的目标。每个子任务都配备了一个独立的分类头。我们提出了一种新颖的多头和多任务学习模型，以有效利用训练数据并利用相关任务训练信号的信息。为了缓解可用训练样本数量有限的问题，我们采用了一种新的去噪自编码器来生成新的合成数据集，用于预训练。此外，我们还提出了一种新的字符级转换方法，以增强序列到编辑功能并提高模型的词汇覆盖率。我们的单模型/集成模型在 BEA-19 (测试集) 和 CoNLL-14 (测试集) 上分别达到了 F0.5 74.4/77.0 和 68.6/69.1。此外，在 JFLEG 测试集上评估时，单模型和集成模型的 GLEU 分数分别为 61.6 和 61.7。这些结果在很大程度上优于最近发布的最新技术成果，且差距显著。

[NLP-78] DocEdit-v2: Document Structure Editing Via Multimodal LLM Grounding EMNLP2024

【速读】： 该论文试图解决文档结构编辑中的关键问题，即如何根据用户请求在文档图像中准确地定位和操作文本、视觉和布局组件。解决方案的关键在于引入DocEdit-v2框架，该框架通过三个核心组件实现端到端的文档编辑：(1) Doc2Command用于同时定位编辑区域并解析用户请求为编辑命令；(2) 基于大语言模型（LLM）的命令重构，将针对专用软件的编辑命令转换为适用于通用大模型的编辑指令；(3) 利用GPT-4V和Gemini等大模态模型处理输出，解析文档布局并执行基于定位区域的编辑，最终生成编辑后的文档图像。实验结果表明，DocEdit-v2在编辑命令生成、区域定位和整体文档编辑任务上显著优于现有基线方法。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16472
作者: Manan Suri,Puneet Mathur,Franck Dernoncourt,Rajiv Jain,Vlad I Morariu,Ramit Sawhney,Preslav Nakov,Dinesh Manocha
关键词-EN: manipulating localized textual, involves manipulating localized, Large Multimodal Models, structure editing involves, editing involves manipulating
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: EMNLP 2024 (Main)

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Document structure editing involves manipulating localized textual, visual, and layout components in document images based on the user’s requests. Past works have shown that multimodal grounding of user requests in the document image and identifying the accurate structural components and their associated attributes remain key challenges for this task. To address these, we introduce the DocEdit-v2, a novel framework that performs end-to-end document editing by leveraging Large Multimodal Models (LMMs). It consists of three novel components: (1) Doc2Command, which simultaneously localizes edit regions of interest (RoI) and disambiguates user edit requests into edit commands; (2) LLM-based Command Reformulation prompting to tailor edit commands originally intended for specialized software into edit instructions suitable for generalist LMMs. (3) Moreover, DocEdit-v2 processes these outputs via Large Multimodal Models like GPT-4V and Gemini, to parse the document layout, execute edits on grounded Region of Interest (RoI), and generate the edited document image. Extensive experiments on the DocEdit dataset show that DocEdit-v2 significantly outperforms strong baselines on edit command generation (2-33%), RoI bounding box detection (12-31%), and overall document editing (1-12%) tasks.
摘要：文档结构编辑涉及根据用户请求操作文档图像中的局部文本、视觉和布局组件。过往的研究表明，在文档图像中对用户请求进行多模态定位，并准确识别结构组件及其相关属性，仍然是该任务的关键挑战。为解决这些问题，我们引入了 DocEdit-v2，这是一个利用大模态模型 (LMMs) 进行端到端文档编辑的新框架。它包含三个创新组件：(1) Doc2Command，该组件同时定位感兴趣的编辑区域 (RoI) 并消除用户编辑请求的歧义，将其转化为编辑命令；(2) 基于大语言模型 (LLM) 的命令重构提示，将原本针对专业软件设计的编辑命令调整为适用于通用大模态模型的编辑指令；(3) 此外，DocEdit-v2 通过 GPT-4V 和 Gemini 等大模态模型处理这些输出，解析文档布局，在定位的感兴趣区域 (RoI) 上执行编辑，并生成编辑后的文档图像。在 DocEdit 数据集上的广泛实验表明，DocEdit-v2 在编辑命令生成 (2-33%)、RoI 边界框检测 (12-31%) 以及整体文档编辑 (1-12%) 任务上显著优于强大的基线模型。

[NLP-79] Beyond Browsing: API-Based Web Agents

【速读】： 该论文试图解决的问题是如何在执行在线任务时，利用API接口替代传统的网页浏览方式，以提高任务执行效率和成功率。解决方案的关键在于提出了两种新型AI代理：(1) 仅通过API调用执行任务的API-calling代理，以及(2) 结合网页浏览和API调用的混合代理。实验结果表明，API-based代理在WebArena基准测试中表现优于传统的网页浏览代理，而混合代理在各项任务中均表现最佳，成功率提升了20.0%以上，达到了当前任务无关代理的SOTA性能。这表明在API可用的情况下，利用API进行任务执行是一种更具吸引力的替代方案。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16464
作者: Yueqi Song,Frank Xu,Shuyan Zhou,Graham Neubig
关键词-EN: web browsing, agents, activity is undertaken, human activity, Web
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Multiagent Systems (cs.MA)
备注: 24 pages, 6 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Web browsers are a portal to the internet, where much of human activity is undertaken. Thus, there has been significant research work in AI agents that interact with the internet through web browsing. However, there is also another interface designed specifically for machine interaction with online content: application programming interfaces (APIs). In this paper we ask – what if we were to take tasks traditionally tackled by browsing agents, and give AI agents access to APIs? To do so, we propose two varieties of agents: (1) an API-calling agent that attempts to perform online tasks through APIs only, similar to traditional coding agents, and (2) a Hybrid Agent that can interact with online data through both web browsing and APIs. In experiments on WebArena, a widely-used and realistic benchmark for web navigation tasks, we find that API-based agents outperform web browsing agents. Hybrid Agents out-perform both others nearly uniformly across tasks, resulting in a more than 20.0% absolute improvement over web browsing alone, achieving a success rate of 35.8%, achiving the SOTA performance among task-agnostic agents. These results strongly suggest that when APIs are available, they present an attractive alternative to relying on web browsing alone.
摘要：网络浏览器是通往互联网的门户，人类活动的大部分都在这里进行。因此，针对通过网页浏览与互联网交互的 AI 智能体，已有大量的研究工作。然而，还有另一种专门为机器与在线内容交互设计的接口：应用程序编程接口 (APIs)。本文提出一个问题——如果我们把传统上由浏览智能体处理的任务，改为让 AI 智能体通过 APIs 来完成，会怎样？为此，我们提出了两种智能体：(1) 一种仅通过 APIs 尝试执行在线任务的 API 调用智能体，类似于传统的编码智能体；(2) 一种可以通过网页浏览和 APIs 与在线数据交互的混合智能体。在广泛使用且真实的网页导航任务基准 WebArena 上的实验中，我们发现基于 API 的智能体优于网页浏览智能体。混合智能体在几乎所有任务中都优于其他两种智能体，相对于仅使用网页浏览，成功率提高了 20.0% 以上，达到了 35.8%，在任务无关的智能体中达到了最先进的性能。这些结果强烈表明，当 APIs 可用时，它们为仅依赖网页浏览提供了一个有吸引力的替代方案。

[NLP-80] Comparative Study of Multilingual Idioms and Similes in Large Language Models

【速读】： 该论文试图解决不同类型比喻语言在多语言环境下的大语言模型（LLMs）解释性能比较的问题。解决方案的关键在于通过多语言数据集（包括新构建的波斯语数据集）评估LLMs在解释明喻和习语时的表现，并探索多种提示工程策略（如思维链、少样本学习和英语翻译提示）的有效性。研究结果表明，提示工程方法在不同比喻类型、语言和模型间的效果存在显著差异，且开源模型在低资源语言的明喻解释中表现较差，而习语解释在许多语言中已接近饱和，需要更具挑战性的评估。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16461
作者: Paria Khoshtab,Danial Namazifard,Mostafa Masoudi,Ali Akhgary,Samin Mahdizadeh Sani,Yadollah Yaghoobzadeh
关键词-EN: study addresses, addresses the gap, English translation prompts, comparative performance, English translation
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 22 pages, 4 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This study addresses the gap in the literature concerning the comparative performance of LLMs in interpreting different types of figurative language across multiple languages. By evaluating LLMs using two multilingual datasets on simile and idiom interpretation, we explore the effectiveness of various prompt engineering strategies, including chain-of-thought, few-shot, and English translation prompts. We extend the language of these datasets to Persian as well by building two new evaluation sets. Our comprehensive assessment involves both closed-source (GPT-3.5, GPT-4o mini, Gemini 1.5), and open-source models (Llama 3.1, Qwen2), highlighting significant differences in performance across languages and figurative types. Our findings reveal that while prompt engineering methods are generally effective, their success varies by figurative type, language, and model. We also observe that open-source models struggle particularly with low-resource languages in similes. Additionally, idiom interpretation is nearing saturation for many languages, necessitating more challenging evaluations.
摘要：本研究针对现有文献中关于大语言模型 (LLM) 在多语言环境下解释不同类型比喻语言的性能比较方面的空白。通过使用两个多语言数据集对隐喻和习语解释进行评估，我们探讨了各种提示工程策略的有效性，包括思维链 (chain-of-thought)、少样本 (few-shot) 和英语翻译提示。我们还将这些数据集的语言扩展到波斯语，构建了两个新的评估集。我们的综合评估涵盖了闭源模型（如 GPT-3.5、GPT-4o mini、Gemini 1.5）和开源模型（如 Llama 3.1、Qwen2），突显了不同语言和比喻类型之间的性能差异。我们的研究结果表明，尽管提示工程方法总体上是有效的，但其成功率因比喻类型、语言和模型而异。我们还观察到，开源模型在处理低资源语言的隐喻时尤其困难。此外，许多语言的习语解释已接近饱和，需要进行更具挑战性的评估。

[NLP-81] o the Globe (TTG): Towards Language-Driven Guaranteed Travel Planning

【速读】： 该论文试图解决旅行规划中复杂且耗时的行程安排问题，特别是如何满足用户在航班、住宿、景点等多个相互依赖的约束条件下的需求。解决方案的关键在于提出了To the Globe (TTG)系统，该系统通过一个经过微调的大型语言模型（LLM）将用户的自然语言请求转换为符号形式，并利用混合整数线性规划（MILP）求解器生成最优的旅行行程。TTG系统能够在约5秒内响应用户请求，并保证提供符合要求的行程安排。为了训练和验证系统，研究团队开发了一个合成数据管道，基于真实世界数据统计生成用户请求和相关旅行信息，从而微调LLM以实现高效的自然语言到符号形式的转换，最终达到91%的精确匹配率和0.979的最优成本比。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16456
作者: Da JU,Song Jiang,Andrew Cohen,Aaron Foss,Sasha Mitts,Arman Zharmagambetov,Brandon Amos,Xian Li,Justine T Kao,Maryam Fazel-Zarandi,Yuandong Tian
关键词-EN: Mixed Integer Linear, Integer Linear Programming, Large Language Model, Linear Programming solvers, satisfies multiple
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Travel planning is a challenging and time-consuming task that aims to find an itinerary which satisfies multiple, interdependent constraints regarding flights, accommodations, attractions, and other travel arrangements. In this paper, we propose To the Globe (TTG), a real-time demo system that takes natural language requests from users, translates it to symbolic form via a fine-tuned Large Language Model, and produces optimal travel itineraries with Mixed Integer Linear Programming solvers. The overall system takes ~5 seconds to reply to the user request with guaranteed itineraries. To train TTG, we develop a synthetic data pipeline that generates user requests, flight and hotel information in symbolic form without human annotations, based on the statistics of real-world datasets, and fine-tune an LLM to translate NL user requests to their symbolic form, which is sent to the symbolic solver to compute optimal itineraries. Our NL-symbolic translation achieves ~91% exact match in a backtranslation metric (i.e., whether the estimated symbolic form of generated natural language matches the groundtruth), and its returned itineraries have a ratio of 0.979 compared to the optimal cost of the ground truth user request. When evaluated by users, TTG achieves consistently high Net Promoter Scores (NPS) of 35-40% on generated itinerary.
摘要：旅行规划是一项具有挑战性且耗时的任务，旨在找到满足多个相互依赖约束条件的行程安排，包括航班、住宿、景点及其他旅行安排。本文提出了“To the Globe”（TTG），这是一个实时演示系统，能够接收用户的自然语言请求，通过微调的大语言模型将其转换为符号形式，并利用混合整数线性规划求解器生成最优的旅行行程。整个系统在约5秒内即可回复用户请求，并保证提供行程安排。为了训练TTG，我们开发了一个合成数据流水线，基于真实世界数据集的统计信息，自动生成用户的请求、航班和酒店信息，无需人工标注。我们微调了一个大语言模型，将自然语言用户请求转换为符号形式，然后将其发送给符号求解器以计算最优行程。我们的自然语言-符号转换在回译度量（即生成的自然语言的估计符号形式是否与真实值匹配）上达到了约91%的精确匹配率，并且其返回的行程与真实用户请求的最优成本相比，成本比率为0.979。在用户评估中，TTG在生成的行程上持续获得高达35-40%的净推荐分数（NPS）。

[NLP-82] Does your LLM truly unlearn? An embarrassingly simple approach to recover unlearned knowledge

【速读】： 该论文试图解决的问题是现有的大语言模型（LLMs）在执行机器遗忘（machine unlearning）后，是否真正实现了知识的彻底遗忘，还是仅仅隐藏了这些知识，而当前的遗忘基准无法检测到这一点。论文通过实验揭示，对已执行遗忘操作的模型应用量化技术后，可以恢复被“遗忘”的信息。解决方案的关键在于提出一种量化鲁棒的遗忘策略，通过理论解释和实验验证，确保在量化过程中仍能有效保持遗忘效果，从而解决量化过程中知识恢复的问题。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16454
作者: Zhiwei Zhang,Fali Wang,Xiaomin Li,Zongyu Wu,Xianfeng Tang,Hui Liu,Qi He,Wenpeng Yin,Suhang Wang
关键词-EN: vast textual corpora, Large language models, shown remarkable proficiency, Large language, generating text
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: 21 pages, 2 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large language models (LLMs) have shown remarkable proficiency in generating text, benefiting from extensive training on vast textual corpora. However, LLMs may also acquire unwanted behaviors from the diverse and sensitive nature of their training data, which can include copyrighted and private content. Machine unlearning has been introduced as a viable solution to remove the influence of such problematic content without the need for costly and time-consuming retraining. This process aims to erase specific knowledge from LLMs while preserving as much model utility as possible. Despite the effectiveness of current unlearning methods, little attention has been given to whether existing unlearning methods for LLMs truly achieve forgetting or merely hide the knowledge, which current unlearning benchmarks fail to detect. This paper reveals that applying quantization to models that have undergone unlearning can restore the “forgotten” information. To thoroughly evaluate this phenomenon, we conduct comprehensive experiments using various quantization techniques across multiple precision levels. We find that for unlearning methods with utility constraints, the unlearned model retains an average of 21% of the intended forgotten knowledge in full precision, which significantly increases to 83% after 4-bit quantization. Based on our empirical findings, we provide a theoretical explanation for the observed phenomenon and propose a quantization-robust unlearning strategy to mitigate this intricate issue…
摘要：大语言模型 (LLMs) 在生成文本方面表现出了显著的熟练度，这得益于其在庞大的文本语料库上的广泛训练。然而，LLMs 也可能从其训练数据的多样性和敏感性中获取不必要的行为，这些数据可能包括受版权保护和私有的内容。机器遗忘 (Machine unlearning) 已被引入作为一种可行的解决方案，以在不进行代价高昂且耗时的重新训练的情况下，消除这些有问题的内容的影响。这一过程旨在从 LLMs 中抹去特定的知识，同时尽可能保留模型的实用性。尽管当前的遗忘方法有效，但很少有人关注现有的 LLMs 遗忘方法是否真正实现了遗忘，还是仅仅隐藏了知识，而当前的遗忘基准无法检测到这一点。本文揭示了将量化应用于已经遗忘的模型可以恢复“被遗忘”的信息。为了彻底评估这一现象，我们使用多种量化技术在多个精度级别上进行了全面的实验。我们发现，对于具有实用性约束的遗忘方法，未学习模型在全精度下平均保留了 21% 的预期遗忘知识，而在 4-bit 量化后显著增加到 83%。基于我们的实证发现，我们为观察到的现象提供了理论解释，并提出了一种量化鲁棒的遗忘策略来缓解这一复杂问题…

[NLP-83] Susu Box or Piggy Bank: Assessing Cultural Commonsense Knowledge between Ghana and the U.S EMNLP2024

【速读】： 该论文试图解决常识知识的文化依赖性问题，特别是英语大型语言模型（LLMs）在不同文化背景下的表现差异。解决方案的关键在于创建了一个名为AMAMMER ε的测试集，该测试集包含525道多选题，旨在评估英语LLMs在加纳和美国文化背景下的常识知识。通过多阶段调查过程，包括参与者编写正确和错误答案、对答案进行评分以及最终验证选择最佳答案，确保了测试项目在不同文化背景下的有效性和一致性。研究结果表明，现有模型更倾向于美国文化背景的答案，但在指定文化背景下，模型表现出一定的适应能力，尤其是在美国文化背景下表现更好。这强调了开发和评估具有文化适应性的模型的重要性，以满足全球多样化英语使用者的需求。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16451
作者: Christabel Acquaye,Haozhe An,Rachel Rudinger
关键词-EN: Recent work, commonsense knowledge, work has highlighted, highlighted the culturally-contingent, culturally-contingent nature
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Accepted to EMNLP 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent work has highlighted the culturally-contingent nature of commonsense knowledge. We introduce AMAMMER \epsilon , a test set of 525 multiple-choice questions designed to evaluate the commonsense knowledge of English LLMs, relative to the cultural contexts of Ghana and the United States. To create AMAMMER \epsilon , we select a set of multiple-choice questions (MCQs) from existing commonsense datasets and rewrite them in a multi-stage process involving surveys of Ghanaian and U.S. participants. In three rounds of surveys, participants from both pools are solicited to (1) write correct and incorrect answer choices, (2) rate individual answer choices on a 5-point Likert scale, and (3) select the best answer choice from the newly-constructed MCQ items, in a final validation step. By engaging participants at multiple stages, our procedure ensures that participant perspectives are incorporated both in the creation and validation of test items, resulting in high levels of agreement within each pool. We evaluate several off-the-shelf English LLMs on AMAMMER \epsilon . Uniformly, models prefer answers choices that align with the preferences of U.S. annotators over Ghanaian annotators. Additionally, when test items specify a cultural context (Ghana or the U.S.), models exhibit some ability to adapt, but performance is consistently better in U.S. contexts than Ghanaian. As large resources are devoted to the advancement of English LLMs, our findings underscore the need for culturally adaptable models and evaluations to meet the needs of diverse English-speaking populations around the world.
摘要：近期研究强调了常识知识的文化依赖性。我们引入了 AMAMMER \epsilon，这是一个包含 525 道多选题的测试集，旨在评估英语大语言模型 (LLM) 相对于加纳和美国文化背景的常识知识。为了创建 AMAMMER \epsilon，我们从现有的常识数据集中选取了一组多选题 (MCQs)，并通过涉及加纳和美国参与者的多阶段调查过程对其进行重写。在三轮调查中，来自两个群体的参与者被要求 (1) 编写正确和错误的答案选项，(2) 对单个答案选项进行 5 点 Likert 量表评分，以及 (3) 从新构建的 MCQ 项目中选择最佳答案选项，作为最终验证步骤。通过在多个阶段与参与者互动，我们的流程确保了参与者视角在测试项目的创建和验证中得到整合，从而在每个群体内实现了高度的一致性。我们对多个现成的英语大语言模型进行了 AMAMMER \epsilon 评估。一致地，模型倾向于选择与美国标注者偏好一致的答案选项，而非加纳标注者。此外，当测试项目明确指定文化背景（加纳或美国）时，模型表现出一定的适应能力，但在美国背景下的表现始终优于加纳背景。随着大量资源投入到英语大语言模型的进步中，我们的研究结果强调了开发和评估具有文化适应性的模型以满足全球多样化英语使用者需求的必要性。

[NLP-84] Improving Neuron-level Interpretability with White-box Language Models

【速读】： 该论文试图解决神经网络模型在神经元层面上的可解释性问题，解决方案的关键在于将稀疏编码直接嵌入到模型架构中，而不是作为事后处理。具体来说，论文提出了一种名为Coding RAte TransformEr (CRATE)的白盒Transformer架构，该架构专门设计用于捕捉数据分布中的稀疏低维结构。通过这种方式，CRATE显著提升了神经元层面的可解释性，实验结果显示在多种评估指标上取得了高达103%的相对改进，且这种改进在不同模型层和大小中保持稳定，表明CRATE在增强神经网络可解释性方面具有鲁棒性能。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16443
作者: Hao Bai,Yi Ma
关键词-EN: Neurons in auto-regressive, auto-regressive language models, activation patterns, auto-regressive language, interpreted by analyzing
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Neurons in auto-regressive language models like GPT-2 can be interpreted by analyzing their activation patterns. Recent studies have shown that techniques such as dictionary learning, a form of post-hoc sparse coding, enhance this neuron-level interpretability. In our research, we are driven by the goal to fundamentally improve neural network interpretability by embedding sparse coding directly within the model architecture, rather than applying it as an afterthought. In our study, we introduce a white-box transformer-like architecture named Coding RAte TransformEr (CRATE), explicitly engineered to capture sparse, low-dimensional structures within data distributions. Our comprehensive experiments showcase significant improvements (up to 103% relative improvement) in neuron-level interpretability across a variety of evaluation metrics. Detailed investigations confirm that this enhanced interpretability is steady across different layers irrespective of the model size, underlining CRATE’s robust performance in enhancing neural network interpretability. Further analysis shows that CRATE’s increased interpretability comes from its enhanced ability to consistently and distinctively activate on relevant tokens. These findings point towards a promising direction for creating white-box foundation models that excel in neuron-level interpretation.
摘要：在像 GPT-2 这样的自回归语言模型中，神经元的解释可以通过分析其激活模式来实现。最近的研究表明，诸如字典学习（一种事后稀疏编码的形式）等技术可以增强这种神经元级别的可解释性。在我们的研究中，我们的目标是根本性地通过将稀疏编码直接嵌入模型架构中来提高神经网络的可解释性，而不是将其作为事后应用。在我们的研究中，我们引入了一种名为 Coding RAte TransformEr (CRATE) 的白盒 Transformer 类架构，该架构明确设计用于捕捉数据分布中的稀疏、低维结构。我们的全面实验展示了在各种评估指标上神经元级别可解释性的显著提升（高达 103% 的相对提升）。详细的调查证实，这种增强的可解释性在不同层之间是稳定的，无论模型大小如何，都突显了 CRATE 在增强神经网络可解释性方面的稳健性能。进一步的分析表明，CRATE 增加的可解释性来自于其增强的能力，即在相关 Token 上持续且显著地激活。这些发现指向了一个有前景的方向，即创建在神经元级别解释上表现优异的白盒基础模型。

[NLP-85] Enhancing Multimodal Affective Analysis with Learned Live Comment Features

【速读】： 该论文试图解决实时弹幕（Danmaku）在情感分析中的应用受限于其在不同视频平台上的稀少性问题。解决方案的关键在于构建了一个名为Live Comment for Affective Analysis (LCAffect)的数据集，该数据集包含多种类型视频的实时弹幕，涵盖广泛的情绪反应。通过对比学习训练视频编码器，生成合成弹幕特征，从而在多模态情感内容分析中显著提升性能，尤其是在情感识别、情绪分类和讽刺检测等任务中表现优异。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16407
作者: Zhaoyuan Deng,Amith Ananthram,Kathleen McKeown
关键词-EN: Live comments, synthetic live comment, live comment features, Live, user-generated messages
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Multimedia (cs.MM)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Live comments, also known as Danmaku, are user-generated messages that are synchronized with video content. These comments overlay directly onto streaming videos, capturing viewer emotions and reactions in real-time. While prior work has leveraged live comments in affective analysis, its use has been limited due to the relative rarity of live comments across different video platforms. To address this, we first construct the Live Comment for Affective Analysis (LCAffect) dataset which contains live comments for English and Chinese videos spanning diverse genres that elicit a wide spectrum of emotions. Then, using this dataset, we use contrastive learning to train a video encoder to produce synthetic live comment features for enhanced multimodal affective content analysis. Through comprehensive experimentation on a wide range of affective analysis tasks (sentiment, emotion recognition, and sarcasm detection) in both English and Chinese, we demonstrate that these synthetic live comment features significantly improve performance over state-of-the-art methods.
摘要：实时评论，又称弹幕，是与视频内容同步的用户生成消息。这些评论直接叠加在流媒体视频上，实时捕捉观众的情感和反应。尽管先前的工作已经利用实时评论进行情感分析，但由于不同视频平台上实时评论的相对稀缺性，其应用受到了限制。为了解决这一问题，我们首先构建了用于情感分析的实时评论数据集 (Live Comment for Affective Analysis, LCAffect)，该数据集包含涵盖多种引发广泛情感反应的英语和中文视频的实时评论。然后，利用该数据集，我们采用对比学习训练视频编码器，以生成增强多模态情感内容分析的合成实时评论特征。通过在英语和中文的广泛情感分析任务（情感、情绪识别和讽刺检测）上的全面实验，我们证明了这些合成实时评论特征显著优于最先进的方法。

[NLP-86] VipAct: Visual-Perception Enhancement via Specialized VLM Agent Collaboration and Tool-use

【速读】： 该论文试图解决视觉-语言模型（VLMs）在细粒度视觉感知任务中表现不足的问题，这些任务需要详细的像素级分析。解决方案的关键在于提出了VipAct框架，该框架通过集成多智能体协作和视觉专家模型，增强了VLMs的视觉理解和综合推理能力。VipAct包括一个协调智能体，负责任务需求分析、规划和协调，以及专门处理特定任务（如图像描述）的智能体和提供高精度感知信息的视觉专家模型。这种多智能体方法通过协同规划、推理和工具使用，使VLMs能够更好地执行细粒度视觉感知任务。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16400
作者: Zhehao Zhang,Ryan Rossi,Tong Yu,Franck Dernoncourt,Ruiyi Zhang,Jiuxiang Gu,Sungchul Kim,Xiang Chen,Zichao Wang,Nedim Lipka
关键词-EN: demonstrated remarkable performance, require detailed pixel-level, tasks combining textual, visual perception, demonstrated remarkable
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:While vision-language models (VLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance across various tasks combining textual and visual information, they continue to struggle with fine-grained visual perception tasks that require detailed pixel-level analysis. Effectively eliciting comprehensive reasoning from VLMs on such intricate visual elements remains an open challenge. In this paper, we present VipAct, an agent framework that enhances VLMs by integrating multi-agent collaboration and vision expert models, enabling more precise visual understanding and comprehensive reasoning. VipAct consists of an orchestrator agent, which manages task requirement analysis, planning, and coordination, along with specialized agents that handle specific tasks such as image captioning and vision expert models that provide high-precision perceptual information. This multi-agent approach allows VLMs to better perform fine-grained visual perception tasks by synergizing planning, reasoning, and tool use. We evaluate VipAct on benchmarks featuring a diverse set of visual perception tasks, with experimental results demonstrating significant performance improvements over state-of-the-art baselines across all tasks. Furthermore, comprehensive ablation studies reveal the critical role of multi-agent collaboration in eliciting more detailed System-2 reasoning and highlight the importance of image input for task planning. Additionally, our error analysis identifies patterns of VLMs’ inherent limitations in visual perception, providing insights into potential future improvements. VipAct offers a flexible and extensible framework, paving the way for more advanced visual perception systems across various real-world applications.
摘要：尽管视觉-语言模型 (Vision-Language Models, VLMs) 在结合文本和视觉信息的多种任务中展现了卓越的性能，但在需要详细像素级分析的细粒度视觉感知任务上仍面临挑战。如何有效地从 VLMs 中引出对这些复杂视觉元素的全面推理，仍然是一个开放的难题。本文提出了 VipAct，这是一个通过集成多智能体协作和视觉专家模型来增强 VLMs 的智能体框架，从而实现更精确的视觉理解和全面的推理。VipAct 包括一个协调智能体，负责任务需求分析、规划和协调，以及处理特定任务（如图像描述）的专用智能体和提供高精度感知信息的视觉专家模型。这种多智能体方法通过协同规划、推理和工具使用，使 VLMs 能够更好地执行细粒度视觉感知任务。我们在包含多样化视觉感知任务的基准上评估了 VipAct，实验结果显示，在所有任务中，VipAct 的性能显著优于最先进的基线。此外，全面的消融研究揭示了多智能体协作在引出更详细的系统 2 推理中的关键作用，并强调了图像输入在任务规划中的重要性。同时，我们的错误分析揭示了 VLMs 在视觉感知中的固有限制，为未来的潜在改进提供了见解。VipAct 提供了一个灵活且可扩展的框架，为各种实际应用中更先进的视觉感知系统铺平了道路。

[NLP-87] LLM-based Optimization of Compound AI Systems: A Survey

【速读】： 该论文试图解决复合AI系统中参数（如指令或工具定义）的端到端优化问题。解决方案的关键在于利用大型语言模型（LLM）作为优化器，通过避免梯度计算并生成复杂的代码和指令，实现对复合AI系统行为的有效优化。论文通过引入程序分析的概念，提供了一个统一的视角，指导LLM如何优化复合AI系统。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16392
作者: Matthieu Lin,Jenny Sheng,Andrew Zhao,Shenzhi Wang,Yang Yue,Yiran Wu,Huan Liu,Jun Liu,Gao Huang,Yong-Jin Liu
关键词-EN: LLM call, LLM, code interpreter, compound, tools are interconnected
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In a compound AI system, components such as an LLM call, a retriever, a code interpreter, or tools are interconnected. The system’s behavior is primarily driven by parameters such as instructions or tool definitions. Recent advancements enable end-to-end optimization of these parameters using an LLM. Notably, leveraging an LLM as an optimizer is particularly efficient because it avoids gradient computation and can generate complex code and instructions. This paper presents a survey of the principles and emerging trends in LLM-based optimization of compound AI systems. It covers archetypes of compound AI systems, approaches to LLM-based end-to-end optimization, and insights into future directions and broader impacts. Importantly, this survey uses concepts from program analysis to provide a unified view of how an LLM optimizer is prompted to optimize a compound AI system. The exhaustive list of paper is provided at this https URL.
摘要：在复合 AI 系统中，大语言模型 (LLM) 调用、检索器、代码解释器或工具等组件相互连接。系统的行为主要由指令或工具定义等参数驱动。近期进展使得可以使用 LLM 对这些参数进行端到端优化。值得注意的是，利用 LLM 作为优化器特别高效，因为它避免了梯度计算，并且能够生成复杂的代码和指令。本文综述了基于 LLM 的复合 AI 系统优化的原理和新兴趋势。内容涵盖复合 AI 系统的原型、基于 LLM 的端到端优化方法，以及对未来方向和更广泛影响的见解。特别地，本综述运用程序分析的概念，提供了一个统一的视角，展示了如何引导 LLM 优化器优化复合 AI 系统。论文的详尽列表可在以下链接中找到：[https URL]。

[NLP-88] KatzBot: Revolutionizing Academic Chatbot for Enhanced Communication

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大学内部沟通中，现有聊天机器人系统无法提供准确、上下文相关的响应，导致用户体验不佳的问题。解决方案的关键在于开发了KatzBot，这是一个由KatzGPT驱动的创新聊天机器人，KatzGPT是一个针对学术领域数据进行微调的大型语言模型（LLM）。KatzGPT基于两个大学特定的数据集进行训练，包括6,280个句子完成对和7,330个问答对，从而显著提高了模型的准确性和领域相关性。KatzBot不仅在性能上超越了现有的开源LLM，还通过用户友好的界面大幅提升了用户满意度。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16385
作者: Sahil Kumar,Deepa Paikar,Kiran Sai Vutukuri,Haider Ali,Shashidhar Reddy Ainala,Aditya Murli Krishnan,Youshan Zhang
关键词-EN: Effective communication, external stakeholders, Large Language Model, communication within universities, universities is crucial
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Effective communication within universities is crucial for addressing the diverse information needs of students, alumni, and external stakeholders. However, existing chatbot systems often fail to deliver accurate, context-specific responses, resulting in poor user experiences. In this paper, we present KatzBot, an innovative chatbot powered by KatzGPT, a custom Large Language Model (LLM) fine-tuned on domain-specific academic data. KatzGPT is trained on two university-specific datasets: 6,280 sentence-completion pairs and 7,330 question-answer pairs. KatzBot outperforms established existing open source LLMs, achieving higher accuracy and domain relevance. KatzBot offers a user-friendly interface, significantly enhancing user satisfaction in real-world applications. The source code is publicly available at \urlthis https URL.
摘要：大学内部的有效沟通对于满足学生、校友和外部利益相关者的多样化信息需求至关重要。然而，现有的聊天机器人系统往往无法提供准确、上下文特定的响应，导致用户体验不佳。本文介绍了 KatzBot，这是一种创新的聊天机器人，由 KatzGPT 驱动，KatzGPT 是一种定制的大语言模型 (Large Language Model, LLM)，经过特定领域学术数据的微调。KatzGPT 基于两个大学特定数据集进行训练：6,280 个句子补全对和 7,330 个问答对。KatzBot 在准确性和领域相关性方面优于现有的开源 LLM，显著提升了实际应用中的用户满意度。源代码公开发布于 \urlthis https URL。

[NLP-89] Feint and Attack: Attention-Based Strategies for Jailbreaking and Protecting LLMs

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大型语言模型（LLMs）在面对语义模糊的提示时可能生成的有害内容问题。解决方案的关键在于引入注意力权重的分布分析，并通过定义新的度量指标（如敏感词的注意力强度、基于注意力的上下文依赖分数和注意力分散熵）来描述这种分布。论文提出了基于注意力的攻击策略（Attention-Based Attack, ABA），通过嵌套攻击提示来转移LLMs的注意力分布，从而利用输入中更无害的部分来吸引模型的注意力。同时，还提出了基于注意力的防御策略（Attention-Based Defense, ABD），通过校准输入提示的注意力分布来增强LLMs的鲁棒性。实验结果表明，这两种策略均能有效提升LLMs的安全性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16327
作者: Rui Pu,Chaozhuo Li,Rui Ha,Zejian Chen,Litian Zhang,Zheng Liu,Lirong Qiu,Xi Zhang
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, Language Models, Large Language, vulnerabilities of Large, Attention Dispersion Entropy
类目: Cryptography and Security (cs.CR); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Jailbreak attack can be used to access the vulnerabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) by inducing LLMs to generate the harmful content. And the most common method of the attack is to construct semantically ambiguous prompts to confuse and mislead the LLMs. To access the security and reveal the intrinsic relation between the input prompt and the output for LLMs, the distribution of attention weight is introduced to analyze the underlying reasons. By using statistical analysis methods, some novel metrics are defined to better describe the distribution of attention weight, such as the Attention Intensity on Sensitive Words (Attn_SensWords), the Attention-based Contextual Dependency Score (Attn_DepScore) and Attention Dispersion Entropy (Attn_Entropy). By leveraging the distinct characteristics of these metrics, the beam search algorithm and inspired by the military strategy “Feint and Attack”, an effective jailbreak attack strategy named as Attention-Based Attack (ABA) is proposed. In the ABA, nested attack prompts are employed to divert the attention distribution of the LLMs. In this manner, more harmless parts of the input can be used to attract the attention of the LLMs. In addition, motivated by ABA, an effective defense strategy called as Attention-Based Defense (ABD) is also put forward. Compared with ABA, the ABD can be used to enhance the robustness of LLMs by calibrating the attention distribution of the input prompt. Some comparative experiments have been given to demonstrate the effectiveness of ABA and ABD. Therefore, both ABA and ABD can be used to access the security of the LLMs. The comparative experiment results also give a logical explanation that the distribution of attention weight can bring great influence on the output for LLMs.
摘要：越狱攻击通过诱导大语言模型 (LLMs) 生成有害内容，从而利用 LLMs 的漏洞。最常见的攻击方法是构建语义模糊的提示，以混淆和误导 LLMs。为了评估 LLMs 的安全性并揭示输入提示与输出之间的内在关系，引入了注意力权重的分布来分析其背后的原因。通过使用统计分析方法，定义了一些新的指标来更好地描述注意力权重的分布，例如敏感词上的注意力强度 (Attn_SensWords)、基于注意力的上下文依赖分数 (Attn_DepScore) 和注意力分散熵 (Attn_Entropy)。利用这些指标的独特特性，结合束搜索算法并借鉴军事策略“佯攻与实攻”，提出了一种名为基于注意力的攻击 (Attention-Based Attack, ABA) 的有效越狱攻击策略。在 ABA 中，嵌套的攻击提示被用来转移 LLMs 的注意力分布，从而使输入中更多无害的部分能够吸引 LLMs 的注意力。此外，受 ABA 启发，还提出了一种有效的防御策略，称为基于注意力的防御 (Attention-Based Defense, ABD)。与 ABA 相比，ABD 通过校准输入提示的注意力分布来增强 LLMs 的鲁棒性。通过一些对比实验，证明了 ABA 和 ABD 的有效性。因此，ABA 和 ABD 都可以用于评估 LLMs 的安全性。对比实验结果还给出了一个逻辑解释，即注意力权重的分布对 LLMs 的输出具有重大影响。

[NLP-90] his Candidate is [MASK]. Letters of Reference and Job Market Outcomes using LLMs

【速读】： 该论文试图解决从保密推荐信中提取情感和其他特征的问题，并验证这些特征对经济学学术就业市场候选人表现的预测能力。解决方案的关键在于采用基于提示的学习策略，利用大型语言模型（LLM）进行情感和特征提取，相较于传统的“词袋”方法，LLM提取的情感测量更能预测就业市场结果。此外，论文通过随机森林模型验证了推荐信的质量和长度对就业市场成功的影响，并发现由导师撰写的推荐信与由其他推荐人撰写的推荐信同样重要。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16325
作者: Fabian Slonimczyk
关键词-EN: prompt-based learning strategy, confidential reference letters, job market, implement a prompt-based, prompt-based learning
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:I implement a prompt-based learning strategy to extract measures of sentiment and other features from confidential reference letters. I show that the contents of reference letters is clearly reflected in the performance of job market candidates in the Economics academic job market. In contrast, applying traditional ``bag-of-words’’ approaches produces measures of sentiment that, while positively correlated to my LLM-based measure, are not predictive of job market outcomes. Using a random forest, I show that both letter quality and length are predictive of success in the job market. Letters authored by advisers appear to be as important as those written by other referees.
摘要：我采用基于提示的学习策略，从保密推荐信中提取情感和其他特征的度量。研究表明，推荐信的内容明显反映在经济学学术就业市场中求职者的表现上。相比之下，应用传统的“词袋”方法产生的情感度量虽然与基于大语言模型 (LLM) 的度量呈正相关，但并不能预测就业市场的结果。通过使用随机森林，我发现信件质量和长度都能预测就业市场的成功。由导师撰写的信件似乎与其他推荐人撰写的信件同样重要。

[NLP-91] SouLLMate: An Application Enhancing Diverse Mental Health Support with Adaptive LLMs Prompt Engineering and RAG Techniques

【速读】： 该论文试图解决现有在线心理健康资源未能充分满足用户需求的问题，特别是如何提供多样化、无歧视、个性化且实时的支持。解决方案的关键在于引入SouLLMate系统，该系统结合了大型语言模型（LLM）技术、链式推理、检索增强生成（RAG）、提示工程和领域知识，具备风险检测和主动引导对话等高级功能，并通过RAG实现个性化档案上传和对话信息提取。此外，论文还提出了关键指标总结（KIS）、主动提问策略（PQS）和堆叠多模型推理（SMMR）等方法，以提升模型性能和用户体验，从而增强心理健康支持技术的可用性和有效性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16322
作者: Qiming Guo,Jinwen Tang,Wenbo Sun,Haoteng Tang,Yi Shang,Wenlu Wang
关键词-EN: individuals’ daily lives, mental health support, issues significantly impact, significantly impact individuals’, impact individuals’ daily
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC)
备注: 26 pages, 19 figures, 8 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Mental health issues significantly impact individuals’ daily lives, yet many do not receive the help they need even with available online resources. This study aims to provide diverse, accessible, stigma-free, personalized, and real-time mental health support through cutting-edge AI technologies. It makes the following contributions: (1) Conducting an extensive survey of recent mental health support methods to identify prevalent functionalities and unmet needs. (2) Introducing SouLLMate, an adaptive LLM-driven system that integrates LLM technologies, Chain, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), prompt engineering, and domain knowledge. This system offers advanced features such as Risk Detection and Proactive Guidance Dialogue, and utilizes RAG for personalized profile uploads and Conversational Information Extraction. (3) Developing novel evaluation approaches for preliminary assessments and risk detection via professionally annotated interview data and real-life suicide tendency data. (4) Proposing the Key Indicator Summarization (KIS), Proactive Questioning Strategy (PQS), and Stacked Multi-Model Reasoning (SMMR) methods to enhance model performance and usability through context-sensitive response adjustments, semantic coherence evaluations, and enhanced accuracy of long-context reasoning in language models. This study contributes to advancing mental health support technologies, potentially improving the accessibility and effectiveness of mental health care globally.
摘要：心理健康问题显著影响个人的日常生活，然而即使有可用的在线资源，许多人仍未能获得所需的帮助。本研究旨在通过尖端的 AI 技术，提供多样化、可访问、无污名、个性化且实时的精神健康支持。其贡献如下：(1) 对近期心理健康支持方法进行广泛调查，以识别常见的功能和未满足的需求。(2) 引入 SouLLMate，一个自适应的大语言模型 (LLM) 驱动的系统，该系统集成了 LLM 技术、Chain、检索增强生成 (RAG)、提示工程和领域知识。该系统提供高级功能，如风险检测和主动引导对话，并利用 RAG 进行个性化档案上传和对话信息提取。(3) 开发新的评估方法，通过专业注释的访谈数据和真实生活中的自杀倾向数据进行初步评估和风险检测。(4) 提出关键指标总结 (KIS)、主动提问策略 (PQS) 和堆叠多模型推理 (SMMR) 方法，通过上下文敏感的响应调整、语义一致性评估和语言模型中长上下文推理的准确性提升，来增强模型性能和可用性。本研究有助于推进心理健康支持技术，可能在全球范围内提高心理健康护理的可及性和有效性。

[NLP-92] An evaluation of LLM code generation capabilities through graded exercises

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大语言模型在生成功能代码方面的评估标准问题，特别是如何客观、无偏地评估这些模型的能力。解决方案的关键在于通过分析现有评估方法，并使用Codewars社区的编程挑战数据对GPT4-o-mini模型进行新评估，发现模型成功率与任务难度、编程语言流行度和挑战发布时间呈正相关。进一步分析表明，模型性能的46.6%可归因于任务难度，37.4%可能与训练数据中的挑战解决方案泄露有关，剩余16%受编程语言影响。这些结果提示当前评估方法可能高估了模型生成功能代码的实际能力。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16292
作者: Álvaro Barbero Jiménez
关键词-EN: natural language descriptions, shown prominent capabilities, Large Language Models, Large Language, Language Models
类目: oftware Engineering (cs.SE); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models have shown prominent capabilities in generating functional code from natural language descriptions. However, a standardized way to evaluate these capabilities in an objective and unbiased manner is still to be found. In this paper we review the current evaluation methods available to this end, and run a new evaluation of the performance of one state-of-the-art model (GPT4-o-mini) in solving curated coding challenges in 8 programming languages, obtained from Codewars, a software development community. Our analysis shows that the chance of success of the model has a positive correlation with the task difficulty, the popularity of the programming language being used and the time elapsed since the publication of the challenge. A further approximate explanatory analysis in terms of high-level features hints that while 46.6% of the model performance could be attributed to task difficulty, a 37.4% seems to be related to leakage of the challenge solutions into the model training set, while the remaining 16% depends on the programming language. These results suggest that current evaluation methodologies might be overestimating the actual skill of Large Language Models for generating functional code.
摘要：大语言模型在根据自然语言描述生成功能代码方面展示了显著的能力。然而，目前仍缺乏一种标准化的方法来客观且无偏见地评估这些能力。本文回顾了现有的评估方法，并对一种最先进的模型（GPT4-o-mini）在解决来自软件开发社区 Codewars 的 8 种编程语言的精选编码挑战中的表现进行了新的评估。我们的分析表明，模型的成功率与任务难度、所使用编程语言的流行度以及挑战发布后的时间流逝呈正相关。进一步从高级特征角度进行的近似解释性分析提示，虽然 46.6% 的模型性能可归因于任务难度，但 37.4% 似乎与挑战解决方案泄露到模型训练集中有关，而剩余的 16% 则取决于编程语言。这些结果表明，当前的评估方法可能高估了大语言模型生成功能代码的实际技能。

[NLP-93] Assessing the Performance of Human-Capable LLMs – Are LLMs Coming for Your Job?

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决自动化大型语言模型（LLM）在帮助台和专业咨询任务中的性能评估问题。解决方案的关键在于开发了一个名为SelfScore的新基准，该基准通过评估代理在问题复杂性和响应有用性方面的表现，实现了对自动化代理与人类工作者性能的比较。SelfScore的评分系统透明且简单，同时研究还探索了检索增强生成（RAG）在特定领域任务中的应用，结果表明结合RAG的自动化LLM代理表现优于未结合的代理，并且所有自动化LLM代理的表现均优于人类对照组。这一研究不仅提供了评估AI在帮助台环境中影响的工具，还强调了在自动化转型过程中需要考虑的伦理问题。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16285
作者: John Mavi,Nathan Summers,Sergio Coronado
关键词-EN: Large Language Model, automated Large Language, Language Model, Large Language, automated LLM agents
类目: Computers and Society (cs.CY); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The current paper presents the development and validation of SelfScore, a novel benchmark designed to assess the performance of automated Large Language Model (LLM) agents on help desk and professional consultation tasks. Given the increasing integration of AI in industries, particularly within customer service, SelfScore fills a crucial gap by enabling the comparison of automated agents and human workers. The benchmark evaluates agents on problem complexity and response helpfulness, ensuring transparency and simplicity in its scoring system. The study also develops automated LLM agents to assess SelfScore and explores the benefits of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for domain-specific tasks, demonstrating that automated LLM agents incorporating RAG outperform those without. All automated LLM agents were observed to perform better than the human control group. Given these results, the study raises concerns about the potential displacement of human workers, especially in areas where AI technologies excel. Ultimately, SelfScore provides a foundational tool for understanding the impact of AI in help desk environments while advocating for ethical considerations in the ongoing transition towards automation.
摘要：本文介绍了 SelfScore 的开发与验证，这是一个新颖的基准测试，旨在评估自动化大语言模型 (LLM) 智能体在帮助台和专业咨询任务中的表现。鉴于 AI 在各行业，特别是在客户服务中的日益融合，SelfScore 填补了一个关键的空白，使得自动化智能体与人类工作者之间的比较成为可能。该基准测试通过评估智能体在问题复杂性和响应有用性方面的表现，确保了评分系统的透明性和简洁性。研究还开发了自动化 LLM 智能体以评估 SelfScore，并探讨了检索增强生成 (RAG) 在特定领域任务中的优势，结果表明，结合 RAG 的自动化 LLM 智能体表现优于未结合的智能体。所有自动化 LLM 智能体的表现均优于人类对照组。鉴于这些结果，研究提出了对人类工作者潜在替代的担忧，特别是在 AI 技术表现优异的领域。最终，SelfScore 提供了一个基础工具，用于理解 AI 在帮助台环境中的影响，同时倡导在向自动化过渡的过程中考虑伦理问题。

[NLP-94] Understanding the Effect of Algorithm Transparency of Model Explanations in Text-to-SQL Semantic Parsing

【速读】： 该论文试图解决AI决策解释性问题，特别是在文本到SQL语义解析任务中，如何通过不同透明度的模型解释来影响用户信任和依赖。解决方案的关键在于设计了三种不同透明度的解释级别，并通过实验发现中等透明度的解释既能保持用户对模型的信任，又能促进用户与模型的有效交互和性能提升。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16283
作者: Daking Rai,Rydia R. Weiland,Kayla Margaret Gabriella Herrera,Tyler H. Shaw,Ziyu Yao
关键词-EN: vital for fostering, Semantic Parsing, Explaining the decisions, Explaining, prediction task called
类目: Information Retrieval (cs.IR); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC)
备注: 15 pages, 18 figure, Preprint

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Explaining the decisions of AI has become vital for fostering appropriate user trust in these systems. This paper investigates explanations for a structured prediction task called text-to-SQL Semantic Parsing'', which translates a natural language question into a structured query language (SQL) program. In this task setting, we designed three levels of model explanation, each exposing a different amount of the model's decision-making details (called algorithm transparency’'), and investigated how different model explanations could potentially yield different impacts on the user experience. Our study with \sim 100 participants shows that (1) the low-/high-transparency explanations often lead to less/more user reliance on the model decisions, whereas the medium-transparency explanations strike a good balance. We also show that (2) only the medium-transparency participant group was able to engage further in the interaction and exhibit increasing performance over time, and that (3) they showed the least changes in trust before and after the study.
摘要：解释 AI 的决策对于促进用户对这些系统的适当信任至关重要。本文研究了针对一个名为“文本到 SQL 语义解析”的结构化预测任务的解释，该任务将自然语言问题转化为结构化查询语言 (SQL) 程序。在此任务设置中，我们设计了三个级别的模型解释，每个级别暴露了不同程度的模型决策细节（称为“算法透明度”），并研究了不同模型解释可能对用户体验产生的不同影响。我们的研究涉及约 100 名参与者，结果显示：(1) 低透明度/高透明度的解释通常导致用户对模型决策的依赖性较低/较高，而中等透明度的解释则达到了良好的平衡。我们还发现：(2) 只有中等透明度的参与者群体能够在交互中进一步参与，并随着时间的推移表现出性能的提升，以及 (3) 他们在研究前后对模型的信任变化最小。

[NLP-95] On Creating an English-Thai Code-switched Machine Translation in Medical Domain

【速读】： 该论文试图解决医学领域机器翻译中术语翻译不准确的问题，关键解决方案是通过代码转换（code-switching, CS）技术，在翻译过程中保留原文中的医学术语，从而提高翻译的准确性和专业性。研究团队开发了一种生成CS医学翻译数据的方法，并使用这些数据微调了一个CS翻译模型，该模型在自动评估和人工偏好测试中均表现出色，尤其受到医学专业人员的青睐。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16221
作者: Parinthapat Pengpun,Krittamate Tiankanon,Amrest Chinkamol,Jiramet Kinchagawat,Pitchaya Chairuengjitjaras,Pasit Supholkhan,Pubordee Aussavavirojekul,Chiraphat Boonnag,Kanyakorn Veerakanjana,Hirunkul Phimsiri,Boonthicha Sae-jia,Nattawach Sataudom,Piyalitt Ittichaiwong,Peerat Limkonchotiwat
关键词-EN: enhancing healthcare quality, disseminating medical knowledge, medical domain plays, Google Neural Machine, domain plays
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Machine translation (MT) in the medical domain plays a pivotal role in enhancing healthcare quality and disseminating medical knowledge. Despite advancements in English-Thai MT technology, common MT approaches often underperform in the medical field due to their inability to precisely translate medical terminologies. Our research prioritizes not merely improving translation accuracy but also maintaining medical terminology in English within the translated text through code-switched (CS) translation. We developed a method to produce CS medical translation data, fine-tuned a CS translation model with this data, and evaluated its performance against strong baselines, such as Google Neural Machine Translation (NMT) and GPT-3.5/GPT-4. Our model demonstrated competitive performance in automatic metrics and was highly favored in human preference evaluations. Our evaluation result also shows that medical professionals significantly prefer CS translations that maintain critical English terms accurately, even if it slightly compromises fluency. Our code and test set are publicly available this https URL.
摘要：机器翻译 (MT) 在医疗领域中发挥着关键作用，有助于提升医疗质量并传播医学知识。尽管英语-泰语机器翻译技术取得了进展，但常见的机器翻译方法在医疗领域往往表现不佳，主要原因是无法准确翻译医学术语。我们的研究不仅注重提高翻译准确性，还通过代码转换 (CS) 翻译在翻译文本中保留医学术语的英文表达。我们开发了一种生成 CS 医学翻译数据的方法，使用这些数据微调了一个 CS 翻译模型，并将其性能与 Google 神经机器翻译 (NMT) 和 GPT-3.5/GPT-4 等强基线模型进行了比较。我们的模型在自动评估指标上表现出色，并在人工偏好评估中获得了高度评价。我们的评估结果还显示，医疗专业人员显著偏好保留关键英文术语的 CS 翻译，即使这稍微牺牲了流畅性。我们的代码和测试集可通过此 https URL 公开获取。

[NLP-96] GenAI Assisting Medical Training

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决护士在静脉穿刺和插管等医疗操作技能学习过程中，由于教师资源有限和任务复杂性高而面临的教学挑战。解决方案的关键在于整合生成式人工智能方法，为学生提供实时的操作反馈，从而帮助他们掌握技能并减轻教育者的教学负担。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16164
作者: Stefan Fritsch,Matthias Tschoepe,Vitor Fortes Rey,Lars Krupp,Agnes Gruenerbl,Eloise Monger,Sarah Travenna
关键词-EN: require precise skills, essential for nurses, nurses and require, require precise, Medical procedures
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 2 pages, 2 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Medical procedures such as venipuncture and cannulation are essential for nurses and require precise skills. Learning this skill, in turn, is a challenge for educators due to the number of teachers per class and the complexity of the task. The study aims to help students with skill acquisition and alleviate the educator’s workload by integrating generative AI methods to provide real-time feedback on medical procedures such as venipuncture and cannulation.
摘要：静脉穿刺和插管等医疗程序对护士来说是必不可少的，需要精确的技能。然而，由于每班教师数量有限以及任务的复杂性，教育者在教授这些技能时面临挑战。本研究旨在通过整合生成式 AI (Generative AI) 方法，为静脉穿刺和插管等医疗程序提供实时反馈，从而帮助学生掌握技能并减轻教育者的工作负担。

[NLP-97] On Uncertainty In Natural Language Processing

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决自然语言处理（NLP）中模型预测的不确定性问题，并提出量化这种不确定性的方法。解决方案的关键在于从语言学、统计学和神经网络的角度对不确定性进行表征，并通过实验设计来减少和量化这种不确定性。论文进一步探讨了在文本分类任务中归纳模型偏差对不确定性的影响，并提出了基于非可交换性保形预测的自然语言生成校准采样方法，以及使用辅助预测器量化大型黑箱语言模型置信度的方法。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.03446
作者: Dennis Ulmer
关键词-EN: increasingly capable systems, natural language processing, decade in deep, capable systems, wide variety
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: PhD thesis

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The last decade in deep learning has brought on increasingly capable systems that are deployed on a wide variety of applications. In natural language processing, the field has been transformed by a number of breakthroughs including large language models, which are used in increasingly many user-facing applications. In order to reap the benefits of this technology and reduce potential harms, it is important to quantify the reliability of model predictions and the uncertainties that shroud their development. This thesis studies how uncertainty in natural language processing can be characterized from a linguistic, statistical and neural perspective, and how it can be reduced and quantified through the design of the experimental pipeline. We further explore uncertainty quantification in modeling by theoretically and empirically investigating the effect of inductive model biases in text classification tasks. The corresponding experiments include data for three different languages (Danish, English and Finnish) and tasks as well as a large set of different uncertainty quantification approaches. Additionally, we propose a method for calibrated sampling in natural language generation based on non-exchangeable conformal prediction, which provides tighter token sets with better coverage of the actual continuation. Lastly, we develop an approach to quantify confidence in large black-box language models using auxiliary predictors, where the confidence is predicted from the input to and generated output text of the target model alone. Comments: PhD thesis Subjects: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG) Cite as: arXiv:2410.03446 [cs.AI] (or arXiv:2410.03446v1 [cs.AI] for this version) https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2410.03446 Focus to learn more arXiv-issued DOI via DataCite
摘要：过去十年，深度学习领域涌现出越来越多功能强大的系统，这些系统被广泛应用于各种应用中。在自然语言处理领域，一系列突破性进展，包括大语言模型，已经彻底改变了该领域，并越来越多地应用于面向用户的应用中。为了充分利用这项技术并减少潜在的危害，量化模型预测的可靠性及其开发过程中所伴随的不确定性显得尤为重要。本论文研究了如何从语言学、统计学和神经学的角度来描述自然语言处理中的不确定性，以及如何通过实验流程的设计来减少和量化这些不确定性。我们进一步通过理论和实证研究，探讨了在文本分类任务中归纳模型偏差对不确定性量化的影响。相应的实验涵盖了三种不同语言（丹麦语、英语和芬兰语）的数据以及一系列不同的不确定性量化方法。此外，我们提出了一种基于非可交换性保形预测的自然语言生成校准采样方法，该方法能够提供更紧密的 Token 集合，并更好地覆盖实际的延续部分。最后，我们开发了一种方法，通过辅助预测器来量化大型黑箱语言模型的置信度，其中置信度仅从目标模型的输入和生成的输出文本中预测。

评论：博士论文
学科：人工智能 (cs.AI); 计算与语言 (cs.CL); 机器学习 (cs.LG)
引用方式：arXiv:2410.03446 [cs.AI]
（或 arXiv:2410.03446v1 [cs.AI] 用于此版本）
https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2410.03446
通过 DataCite 发布的 arXiv-issued DOI

[NLP-98] Learning Machines: In Search of a Concept Oriented Language

【速读】： 该论文试图解决下一代“智能”机器应具备何种能力的问题，特别是关于机器如何进行知识发现、决策和概念处理。解决方案的关键在于提出一个面向概念的语言框架，通过类比人类智能的历史贡献，探讨机器智能的核心要素，从而为构建更智能的机器提供理论基础。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.01968
作者: Veyis Gunes
关键词-EN: digital revolution, Abstract, data, digital, revolution
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 17 pages, 8 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:What is the next step after the data/digital revolution? What do we need the most to reach this aim? How machines can memorize, learn or discover? What should they be able to do to be qualified as “intelligent”? These questions relate to the next generation “intelligent” machines. Probably, these machines should be able to handle knowledge discovery, decision-making and concepts. In this paper, we will take into account some historical contributions and discuss these different questions through an analogy to human intelligence. Also, a general framework for a concept oriented language will be proposed.
摘要：数据/数字革命之后，下一步是什么？为了实现这一目标，我们最需要的是什么？机器如何记忆、学习或发现？它们应该具备哪些能力才能被称为“智能”？这些问题与下一代“智能”机器息息相关。或许，这些机器应具备处理知识发现、决策制定和概念的能力。本文将回顾一些历史贡献，并通过类比人类智能来探讨这些问题。此外，还将提出一个面向概念的语言的通用框架。

[NLP-99] Automated Spinal MRI Labelling from Reports Using a Large Language Model MICCAI2024

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决从放射学报告中自动提取标签的问题，特别是针对脊柱MRI报告。解决方案的关键在于利用大型语言模型（如GPT-4）自动化提取五种特定病症（脊柱癌、狭窄、脊柱滑脱、马尾神经压迫和椎间盘突出）的标签，并通过这些自动提取的标签训练影像分类模型，使其性能与使用临床医生手动标注的扫描训练的模型相当。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17235
作者: Robin Y. Park,Rhydian Windsor,Amir Jamaludin,Andrew Zisserman
关键词-EN: spinal MRI reports, spinal MRI, large language models, MRI reports, propose a general
类目: Image and Video Processing (eess.IV); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
备注: Accepted to Medical Image Computing and Computer Assisted Intervention (MICCAI 2024, Spotlight). 11 pages plus appendix

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We propose a general pipeline to automate the extraction of labels from radiology reports using large language models, which we validate on spinal MRI reports. The efficacy of our labelling method is measured on five distinct conditions: spinal cancer, stenosis, spondylolisthesis, cauda equina compression and herniation. Using open-source models, our method equals or surpasses GPT-4 on a held-out set of reports. Furthermore, we show that the extracted labels can be used to train imaging models to classify the identified conditions in the accompanying MR scans. All classifiers trained using automated labels achieve comparable performance to models trained using scans manually annotated by clinicians. Code can be found at this https URL.
摘要：我们提出了一种通用流程，利用大语言模型 (Large Language Model) 来自动从放射学报告中提取标签，并在脊柱 MRI 报告中进行了验证。我们对五种不同的病症进行了标签提取方法的有效性测量：脊柱癌、狭窄、脊柱滑脱、马尾神经压迫和椎间盘突出。使用开源模型，我们的方法在保留的报告集上与 GPT-4 相当或超越。此外，我们展示了提取的标签可以用于训练影像模型，以分类伴随的 MR 扫描中识别出的病症。所有使用自动化标签训练的分类器，其性能与使用临床医生手动注释的扫描训练的模型相当。代码可在以下链接找到：https URL。

[NLP-100] Can a Machine Distinguish High and Low Amount of Social Creak in Speech?

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决社会喉音（social creak）在女性说话者中日益普遍的问题，并探索利用机器学习（ML）技术来自动区分低和高程度的社会喉音。解决方案的关键在于使用机器学习模型，结合梅尔频谱图和梅尔频率倒谱系数等声学特征，训练出能够有效分类不同程度社会喉音的系统。研究结果表明，基于Adaboost分类器和决策树分类器的系统在区分低和高程度社会喉音方面表现最佳，准确率达到了71.1%。这一研究为未来基于机器学习的社会喉音研究提供了基线参考。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17028
作者: Anne-Maria Laukkanen,Sudarsana Reddy Kadiri,Shrikanth Narayanan,Paavo Alku
关键词-EN: social creak, creak, social, ncreased prevalence, speech
类目: Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Sound (cs.SD)
备注: Accepted in Journal of Voice

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Objectives: ncreased prevalence of social creak particularly among female speakers has been reported in several studies. The study of social creak has been previously conducted by combining perceptual evaluation of speech with conventional acoustical parameters such as the harmonic-to-noise ratio and cepstral peak prominence. In the current study, machine learning (ML) was used to automatically distinguish speech of low amount of social creak from speech of high amount of social creak. Methods: The amount of creak in continuous speech samples produced in Finnish by 90 female speakers was first perceptually assessed by two voice specialists. Based on their assessments, the speech samples were divided into two categories (low vs . high amount of creak). Using the speech signals and their creak labels, seven different ML models were trained. Three spectral representations were used as feature for each model. Results: The results show that the best performance (accuracy of 71.1%) was obtained by the following two systems: an Adaboost classifier using the mel-spectrogram feature and a decision tree classifier using the mel-frequency cepstral coefficient feature. Conclusions: The study of social creak is becoming increasingly popular in sociolinguistic and vocological research. The conventional human perceptual assessment of the amount of creak is laborious and therefore ML technology could be used to assist researchers studying social creak. The classification systems reported in this study could be considered as baselines in future ML-based studies on social creak. Comments: Accepted in Journal of Voice Subjects: Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Sound (cs.SD) Cite as: arXiv:2410.17028 [eess.AS] (or arXiv:2410.17028v1 [eess.AS] for this version) https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2410.17028 Focus to learn more arXiv-issued DOI via DataCite (pending registration) Submission history From: Sudarsana Kadiri [view email] [v1] Tue, 22 Oct 2024 13:52:51 UTC (148 KB)
摘要：
目标：多项研究表明，女性说话者中社交性颤音（social creak）的流行度有所增加。以往对社交性颤音的研究通常结合语音的感知评估与传统的声学参数，如谐噪比（harmonic-to-noise ratio）和倒谱峰值突出度（cepstral peak prominence）。在本研究中，机器学习（ML）被用于自动区分低量社交性颤音与高量社交性颤音的语音。

方法：首先，由两名语音专家对90名芬兰女性说话者产生的连续语音样本中的颤音量进行感知评估。根据他们的评估，语音样本被分为两类（低量 vs. 高量颤音）。利用这些语音信号及其颤音标签，训练了七种不同的ML模型。每个模型使用了三种频谱表示作为特征。

结果：结果显示，以下两个系统的性能最佳（准确率为71.1%）：使用梅尔频谱图特征的Adaboost分类器和使用梅尔频率倒谱系数特征的决策树分类器。

结论：社交性颤音的研究在社会语言学和语音学研究中越来越受欢迎。传统的人类感知评估颤音量的方法费时费力，因此ML技术可以用于辅助研究社交性颤音。本研究报告的分类系统可以作为未来基于ML的社交性颤音研究的基础。

评论：已接受发表于《Journal of Voice》。
主题：音频与语音处理（eess.AS）；人工智能（cs.AI）；计算与语言（cs.CL）；机器学习（cs.LG）；声音（cs.SD）
引用方式：arXiv:2410.17028 [eess.AS]（或 arXiv:2410.17028v1 [eess.AS] 用于此版本）
https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2410.17028

From: Sudarsana Kadiri [view email]
[v1] Tue, 22 Oct 2024 13:52:51 UTC (148 KB)

[NLP-101] Enhancing Low-Resource ASR through Versatile TTS: Bridging the Data Gap

【速读】： 该论文试图解决低资源环境下自动语音识别（ASR）系统的性能不足问题，特别是在方言、口音、少数民族语言和长尾热词等实际应用中。解决方案的关键在于利用多功能且强大的文本到语音（TTS）模型进行数据增强，通过生成具有人类水平自然度、表现力和多样性说话者特征的语音数据，以低成本且实用的方式提升ASR系统的性能。实验结果表明，这种方法在多种低资源数据集上均显著提升了ASR性能，并首次系统研究了文本多样性、说话者多样性和合成数据量对ASR改进的影响，为TTS数据增强在低资源ASR中的应用提供了有价值的指导和参考。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16726
作者: Guanrou Yang,Fan Yu,Ziyang Ma,Zhihao Du,Zhifu Gao,Shiliang Zhang,Xie Chen
关键词-EN: efficacy remains inadequate, automatic speech recognition, significant practical relevance, achieved remarkable performance, encompassing dialects
类目: Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:While automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems have achieved remarkable performance with large-scale datasets, their efficacy remains inadequate in low-resource settings, encompassing dialects, accents, minority languages, and long-tail hotwords, domains with significant practical relevance. With the advent of versatile and powerful text-to-speech (TTS) models, capable of generating speech with human-level naturalness, expressiveness, and diverse speaker profiles, leveraging TTS for ASR data augmentation provides a cost-effective and practical approach to enhancing ASR performance. Comprehensive experiments on an unprecedentedly rich variety of low-resource datasets demonstrate consistent and substantial performance improvements, proving that the proposed method of enhancing low-resource ASR through a versatile TTS model is highly effective and has broad application prospects. Furthermore, we delve deeper into key characteristics of synthesized speech data that contribute to ASR improvement, examining factors such as text diversity, speaker diversity, and the volume of synthesized data, with text diversity being studied for the first time in this work. We hope our findings provide helpful guidance and reference for the practical application of TTS-based data augmentation and push the advancement of low-resource ASR one step further.
摘要：尽管自动语音识别 (ASR) 系统在大规模数据集上取得了显著的性能，但在低资源环境下，包括方言、口音、少数民族语言和长尾热词等具有重要实际意义的领域，其有效性仍然不足。随着多功能且强大的文本到语音 (TTS) 模型的出现，这些模型能够生成具有人类水平自然度、表现力和多样化发音特征的语音，利用 TTS 进行 ASR 数据增强提供了一种成本效益高且实用的方法来提升 ASR 性能。在多种前所未有的丰富低资源数据集上的综合实验表明，性能提升持续且显著，证明了通过多功能 TTS 模型增强低资源 ASR 的方法具有高度有效性和广泛的应用前景。此外，我们深入探讨了合成语音数据的关键特征，这些特征有助于 ASR 改进，考察了文本多样性、发音多样性以及合成数据量等因素，其中文本多样性是首次在本研究中进行探讨。我们希望我们的发现能为基于 TTS 的数据增强在实际应用中提供有益的指导和参考，并进一步推动低资源 ASR 的发展。

[NLP-102] End-to-End Transformer-based Automatic Speech Recognition for Northern Kurdish: A Pioneering Approach

【速读】： 该论文试图解决低资源语言（如北库尔德语Kurmanji）的自动语音识别（ASR）问题，由于训练数据有限，这一任务具有挑战性。解决方案的关键在于利用预训练的ASR模型Whisper，并通过三种微调策略（普通微调、特定参数微调和附加模块微调）进行优化。实验结果表明，附加模块微调策略显著提升了ASR准确性，在专用测试集上实现了10.5%的词错误率（WER）和5.7%的字符错误率（CER），突显了复杂变换器模型在低资源ASR中的潜力以及定制微调技术的重要性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.16330
作者: Abdulhady Abas Abdullah,Shima Tabibian,Hadi Veisi,Aso Mahmudi,Tarik Rashid
关键词-EN: Automatic Speech Recognition, challenging task due, limited training data, Automatic Speech, Speech Recognition
类目: Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) for low-resource languages remains a challenging task due to limited training data. This paper introduces a comprehensive study exploring the effectiveness of Whisper, a pre-trained ASR model, for Northern Kurdish (Kurmanji) an under-resourced language spoken in the Middle East. We investigate three fine-tuning strategies: vanilla, specific parameters, and additional modules. Using a Northern Kurdish fine-tuning speech corpus containing approximately 68 hours of validated transcribed data, our experiments demonstrate that the additional module fine-tuning strategy significantly improves ASR accuracy on a specialized test set, achieving a Word Error Rate (WER) of 10.5% and Character Error Rate (CER) of 5.7% with Whisper version 3. These results underscore the potential of sophisticated transformer models for low-resource ASR and emphasize the importance of tailored fine-tuning techniques for optimal performance.
摘要：针对低资源语言的自动语音识别 (ASR) 由于训练数据有限，仍然是一个具有挑战性的任务。本文介绍了一项综合研究，探讨了预训练的 ASR 模型 Whisper 在北库尔德语 (Kurmanji) 这一中东地区资源匮乏语言中的有效性。我们研究了三种微调策略：普通微调、特定参数微调和附加模块微调。使用包含约 68 小时已验证转录数据的北库尔德语微调语音语料库，我们的实验表明，附加模块微调策略显著提高了在专用测试集上的 ASR 准确性，Whisper 版本 3 实现了 10.5% 的词错误率 (WER) 和 5.7% 的字符错误率 (CER)。这些结果突显了复杂 Transformer 模型在低资源 ASR 中的潜力，并强调了定制微调技术对实现最佳性能的重要性。

人工智能

[AI-0] JMMMU: A Japanese Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding Benchmark for Culture-aware Evaluation

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17250
作者: Shota Onohara,Atsuyuki Miyai,Yuki Imajuku,Kazuki Egashira,Jeonghun Baek,Xiang Yue,Graham Neubig,Kiyoharu Aizawa
关键词-EN: Large Multimodal Models, Multimodal Models, Large Multimodal, enhancing user experiences, Accelerating research
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
*备注: Project page: this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Accelerating research on Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) in non-English languages is crucial for enhancing user experiences across broader populations. In this paper, we introduce JMMMU (Japanese MMMU), the first large-scale Japanese benchmark designed to evaluate LMMs on expert-level tasks based on the Japanese cultural context. To facilitate comprehensive culture-aware evaluation, JMMMU features two complementary subsets: (i) culture-agnostic (CA) subset, where the culture-independent subjects (e.g., Math) are selected and translated into Japanese, enabling one-to-one comparison with its English counterpart MMMU; and (ii) culture-specific (CS) subset, comprising newly crafted subjects that reflect Japanese cultural context. Using the CA subset, we observe performance drop in many LMMs when evaluated in Japanese, which is purely attributable to language variation. Using the CS subset, we reveal their inadequate Japanese cultural understanding. Further, by combining both subsets, we identify that some LMMs perform well on the CA subset but not on the CS subset, exposing a shallow understanding of the Japanese language that lacks depth in cultural understanding. We hope this work will not only help advance LMM performance in Japanese but also serve as a guideline to create high-standard, culturally diverse benchmarks for multilingual LMM development. The project page is this https URL.

[AI-1] HyperspectralViTs: Fast and Accurate methane detection on-board satellites

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.17248
作者: Vít Růžička,Andrew Markham
关键词-EN: enable unprecedented amount, machine learning models, machine learning, automated detection on-board, models
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
*备注: 13 pages, This work has been submitted for possible publication

点击查看摘要

Abstract:On-board processing of hyperspectral data with machine learning models would enable unprecedented amount of autonomy for a wide range of tasks, for example methane detection or mineral identification.
